items.title: Audit filter SQL builder (where-condition composition)
items.notes: buildWhereConditions AND-composes equality filters (eventType, category, action, outcome, user, userType, resourceType, resource, retentionStatus, ipAddress) plus date-range bounds (createdAt >= startDate, createdAt <= endDate). Returns undefined when no filters — drives both findMany and count consistently.

items.title: SHA-256 integrity hash for tamper detection
items.notes: calculateIntegrityHash sorts keys then joins key:value pairs with '|' and SHA-256 digests over {eventType, category, action, outcome, user, resourceType, resource, description, timestamp(ISO createdAt)}. Same field set + same timestamp must be used at verify time or the entry reads as compromised.

items.title: Audit log access restricted to admin or compliance role
items.notes: 401 UnauthorizedError if no authenticated user; role string is comma-split and must include 'admin' or 'compliance' else 403 ForbiddenError. Authz boundary on a sensitive compliance surface => inter-module/clinical class layers.

items.title: startDate must not be after endDate
items.notes: When both dates present and startDate > endDate, throws ValidationError 'startDate cannot be after endDate'. Equal dates and single-sided ranges are allowed. Date strings coerced to Date before comparison.

items.title: Audit entry dates serialized to ISO-8601 (nullable archivedAt/purgeAfter)
items.notes: Response maps createdAt/updatedAt to ISO strings; archivedAt/purgeAfter to ISO string when present else null — matches TypeSpec AuditLogEntry / StrictUtcDateTime contract.

items.title: Audit query filters restricted to an explicit allowlist
items.notes: parseFilters only honors eventType, category, action, outcome, user, userType, resourceType, resource, retentionStatus, startDate, endDate, ipAddress. Unrecognised params (incl. injection-style keys like __proto__/evil) are dropped before reaching the repo — security guard against filter injection.

items.title: Audit listing pagination defaults (limit 25) and max-limit clamp (100)
items.notes: parsePagination with audit-specific { limit: 25, maxLimit: 100 }: no params => limit 25 offset 0; oversized limit clamped to 100; custom limit/offset forwarded. Pagination meta (limit, offset, totalCount, count, hasMore) built from findMany + count run in parallel.

items.title: Reading audit logs is itself audit-logged (self-logging)
items.notes: After a successful query the handler writes a data-access/hipaa/read audit_log_entry (resourceType 'audit_log', resource 'audit_log_list', user = caller). Closes the watch-the-watchers loop — every access to the audit trail leaves its own trail.

items.title: Archive active logs older than 1 year (active -> archived)
items.notes: archiveOldLogs(daysOld=365): bulk UPDATE flips retentionStatus active->archived where createdAt <= now-daysOld, stamping archivedAt + archivedBy + updatedAt. Returns count of rows archived. HIPAA 1-year active retention window.

items.title: Audit trail is append-only and is NEVER purged
items.notes: The audit trail is append-only and is NEVER deleted. The cron job audit.retention archives logs older than 1 year (active->archived) but performs NO physical deletes (plan 016 removed the purge step). The 'archived' status is terminal — no rows are ever deleted from audit_log_entry. See handlers/retention/retention-targets.ts (protected/retain) and DATA_GOVERNANCE.md §2/§3/§7.

items.title: Audit retention lifecycle FSM (active -> archived; terminal — never purged)
items.notes: retentionStatus enum {active, archived, pending-purge}; new rows default 'active'. Forward-only transitions: logEvent sets active; archiveOldLogs active->archived; 'archived' is terminal (append-only trail — no deletion ever, plan 016). The 'pending-purge' enum value is retained in the schema for historical compatibility but the cron never transitions rows to it. No backward transition. Filterable via retentionStatus query param + buildWhereConditions.

items.title: Capture/Void: provider-owner (merchant) or admin only
items.notes: captureInvoicePayment.ts:57-83 / voidInvoice.ts:57-72 — parseUserRoles; non-admin must resolve to a billing party whose id === invoice.merchant else 403. The provider, not the patient, decides capture vs void.

items.title: Capture: payment must be in requires_capture (authorized) state
items.notes: captureInvoicePayment.ts:86 already 'succeeded' → 409 ('already been captured'); :91 'canceled' → 409 ('already been voided'); :96 not 'requires_capture' → 422 PAYMENT_NOT_AUTHORIZED; :108 providerDecision already set → 409 ('decision already made'); :112 no stripePaymentIntentId → 422 PAYMENT_INTENT_MISSING; :130 no merchant stripeAccountId → 422 STRIPE_ACCOUNT_MISSING. FSM + double-decision guards.

items.title: Create invoice requires >=1 line item
items.notes: createInvoice.ts:91 — empty/missing lineItems → 422 ValidationError ('At least one line item is required'). Conceptual sibling of dental-billing BR-009 (>=1 line item) on the generic invoice.

items.title: Create invoice: merchant + customer persons must exist
items.notes: createInvoice.ts:66-83 — findBillingParty for both merchant and customer; either missing → 404 NotFoundError. Inter-module read against person.

items.title: Create invoice: merchant must be the caller
items.notes: createInvoice.ts:86 — if merchant !== user.id → 403 ForbiddenError ('You can only create invoices for your own merchant account'). Privilege/tenancy guard preventing creating invoices on another merchant's book.

items.title: Create invoice: duplicate context rejected
items.notes: createInvoice.ts:96-102 — if a context is supplied and an invoice already exists for it → 409 ConflictError ('Invoice with this context already exists'). Idempotency/dedup guard keyed on caller-supplied context (e.g. a booking id).

items.title: Create merchant account: self-only + one per person
items.notes: createMerchantAccount.ts:52 person not found → 404; :61 person.id !== user.id → 403 ('only create merchant accounts for yourself'); :79 existing account for person → 409 ('Person already has a merchant account'). One merchant account per person, owner-only.

items.title: Delete invoice: merchant-owner only + draft-only
items.notes: deleteInvoice.ts:67 merchantPerson.id !== user.id → 403; deleteInvoice.ts:72 status !== 'draft' → 422 INVALID_INVOICE_STATUS ('only draft invoices can be deleted'). Issued/paid/void invoices can never be deleted (audit-integrity).

items.title: Finalize: draft-only FSM + positive-total + merchant-owner
items.notes: finalizeInvoice.ts:69 merchantPerson.id !== user.id → 403; :74 status !== 'draft' → 422 INVALID_INVOICE_STATUS; :82 total <= 0 → 422 INCOMPLETE_INVOICE_DATA. Cannot issue a zero/negative invoice; only draft can be finalized.

items.title: Get invoice: caller must be merchant, customer, or admin
items.notes: getInvoice.ts:71 — invoice.merchant !== user.id && invoice.customer !== user.id && !isAdmin → 403. Ownership boundary; the generic invoice table has no tenant column/RLS so this in-handler guard IS the isolation.

items.title: Sequential invoice number generation (repo)
items.notes: billing.repo.ts:144 generateInvoiceNumber — derives next number by max(invoiceNumber) for the prefix and increments. createWithLineItems (repo:196) inserts invoice + line items in a single transaction. Money-integrity primitive.

items.title: Invoice tax is 0 on create (ADR-011, tax out of scope v1)
items.notes: createInvoice.ts:119 — tax hardcoded 0 per docs/decisions/ADR-011-invoice-tax-out-of-scope-v1.md; total = subtotal. NOTE drift vs updateInvoice which recomputes tax from config.billing.taxRatePct (updateInvoice.ts:122-134) — divergent tax handling between create and update.

items.title: List invoices: non-admins scoped to their own party set (P1-1)
items.notes: listInvoices.ts:68-86 — P1-1 fix: generic invoice table has no tenant/RLS. Non-admin: ?merchant or ?customer must equal user.id else 403; if neither supplied, filter forced to merchant=user.id. Prevents enumerating any provider's book or paging system-wide.

items.title: Onboard: owner-only + account must be active
items.notes: onboardMerchantAccount.ts:65 person !== user.id → 403; :70 !active → 422 MERCHANT_ACCOUNT_INACTIVE. Stripe-not-configured surfaces as ExternalServiceError (502-class).

items.title: Pay invoice: only the customer (patient:owner) may pay
items.notes: payInvoice.ts:88 invoice.customer !== user.id → 403 ('You can only pay your own invoices'). Trust-boundary guard so a patient cannot pay (and thereby manipulate) another patient's invoice.

items.title: Pay invoice: merchant must have completed Stripe onboarding
items.notes: payInvoice.ts:98-113 — merchant account missing → 404; metadata missing stripeAccountId OR onboardingComplete false → 422 PROVIDER_BILLING_INCOMPLETE. Cannot route funds to an un-onboarded merchant.

items.title: Pay invoice: reject if a payment already exists
items.notes: payInvoice.ts:93 paymentStatus set and != 'pending' → 409 ConflictError ('Payment already exists for this invoice'). Prevents creating a second payment intent / double-charge.

items.title: Pay invoice: payment method id must be pm_* format
items.notes: payInvoice.ts:48 paymentMethod present but not starting 'pm_' → 422 ValidationError (Stripe id-format defense).

items.title: Hide issue/void/discount/uncollectible affordances for non-owner billing roles

userEntryPoint.control: footer action buttons (Issue/Void/Apply Discount/Mark Uncollectible) conditional on canWrite/canDiscount/canVoidPmt

items.notes: J-RBAC-001. canWriteBilling(role) = owner/associate gates Issue/Void/Uncollectible/CreatePlan/NewClaim; canApplyDiscount + canVoidPayment = owner-only (stricter) gate Discount/Void-payment/Refund. staff_full/billing_staff can Record Payment only. Backend is hard 403 gate; FE hides affordances. Role from org-context store.

items.title: Hide issue/void/discount/uncollectible affordances for non-owner billing roles

userEntryPoint.control: footer action buttons (Issue/Void/Apply Discount/Mark Uncollectible) conditional on canWrite/canDiscount/canVoidPmt

items.notes: J-RBAC-001. canWriteBilling(role) = owner/associate gates Issue/Void/Uncollectible/CreatePlan/NewClaim; canApplyDiscount + canVoidPayment = owner-only (stricter) gate Discount/Void-payment/Refund. staff_full/billing_staff can Record Payment only. Backend is hard 403 gate; FE hides affordances. Role from org-context store.

items.title: Refund: provider-owner (merchant) or admin only
items.notes: refundInvoicePayment.ts:60-75 — non-admin must be the merchant on the invoice else 403 ('You do not have permission to refund this invoice').

items.title: Refund: requires captured payment, no double-refund, amount capped to total
items.notes: refundInvoicePayment.ts:78 paymentStatus != 'succeeded' → 422 PAYMENT_NOT_CAPTURED; :92 prior refundAmount>0 or refundStatus set → 409 ('already been refunded'); :96/:103 missing intent/charge → 422; :112 refund clamped to min(amount, invoice.total); :114 <=0 → 422. Money invariant: refund never exceeds the invoice total, and only one refund.

items.title: Restrict /billing route to roles permitted the billing area
items.notes: Route-level guard via requireRole('billing') in beforeLoad redirects unauthorized roles before the page loads.

items.title: Restrict /billing route to roles permitted the billing area
items.notes: Route-level guard via requireRole('billing') in beforeLoad redirects unauthorized roles before the page loads.

items.title: Uncollectible: open-only + merchant-owner + not already paid
items.notes: markInvoiceUncollectible.ts:72 merchantPerson.id !== user.id → 403; :77 status !== 'open' → 422 INVALID_INVOICE_STATUS; :85 paymentStatus === 'succeeded' → 422 INVOICE_ALREADY_PAID. Cannot write off a paid invoice or a non-issued draft.

items.title: Update invoice: merchant-owner only + draft-only
items.notes: updateInvoice.ts:77 merchantPerson.id !== user.id → 403; updateInvoice.ts:82 status !== 'draft' → 422 INVALID_INVOICE_STATUS ('only draft invoices can be modified'). Immutability after finalize. Also empty lineItems array → 422 (updateInvoice.ts:117).

items.title: Void: payment must be in requires_capture; cannot void captured
items.notes: voidInvoice.ts:75 'canceled' → 409 ('already been voided'); :80 'succeeded' → 409 ('already captured and cannot be voided'); :85 not 'requires_capture' → 422 PAYMENT_NOT_AUTHORIZED; :97 providerDecision set → 409; :101 no intent → 422 PAYMENT_INTENT_MISSING; :119 no stripeAccountId → 422 STRIPE_ACCOUNT_MISSING. A captured payment must be refunded, not voided.

items.title: Webhook: processing failure surfaces as retryable 5xx (no silent loss)
items.notes: handleStripeWebhook.ts:154-173 — a per-event processing error throws AppError WEBHOOK_PROCESSING_ERROR 500 (not 200, not 422) so Stripe re-delivers; a failed event is NOT marked processed. 'Safe ignore' cases (missing invoice / metadata) return early inside per-event handlers and never reach the catch. Prevents acknowledging-then-dropping a real payment event.

items.title: Webhook idempotency: each event processed once (processed-event ledger)
items.notes: handleStripeWebhook.ts:62 ProcessedWebhookEventRepository.isProcessed(event.id) short-circuits a re-delivery -> {received:true, duplicate:true} 200; :149 markProcessed AFTER success (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, migration 0108 processed_webhook_event). Stripe is at-least-once; the ledger prevents double-firing side effects. Known narrow non-idempotent window: notifications not yet dedup-keyed (money state is absolute-assignment, unaffected).

items.title: Webhook: valid Stripe signature required
items.notes: handleStripeWebhook.ts:30 missing stripe-signature header → 400 ValidationError; :42 verifyWebhookSignature failure → 400 ('Invalid webhook signature'). The signature IS the auth boundary for this unauthenticated endpoint. A bad signature is a 400 and is NOT retried.

items.title: BR: booking access limited to its client or host
items.notes: getBooking allows access only to the booking's client or host (403 otherwise). listBookings rejects host/client filters that aren't the caller and otherwise restricts results to clientOrHost = user.id. (Note: utils/authorization.checkBookingOwnership also encodes admin/support bypass, but the read handlers use the stricter utils/ownership variant.)

items.title: BR: cancellation reason required and <=500 chars
items.notes: cancelBooking: empty/whitespace reason => ValidationError; reason >500 chars => ValidationError. Reject reason is optional but also capped at 500. Reason is persisted as cancellationReason and trimmed.

items.title: BR: only host can confirm/reject; pending-only reject
items.notes: checkBookingHostOwnership gates confirm and reject (non-host => 403). reject additionally requires status='pending' (BusinessLogicError INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION otherwise). FSM: pending -> confirmed | rejected.

items.title: BR: 15-minute confirmation window before auto-reject
items.notes: Pending bookings unconfirmed 15 minutes after bookedAt are auto-rejected by the timer job. Eligibility requires status='pending' AND null confirmationTimestamp. Job re-checks status='pending' inside the transaction to avoid clobbering a just-confirmed booking (concurrency guard).

items.title: BR: booking event configuration validation
items.notes: validateEventConfig + processAndValidateDailyConfigs enforce: maxBookingDays 0-365; minBookingMinutes 0-4320; >=1 locationType; IANA timezone format; status in draft/active/paused/archived; per time block HH:MM format, startTime<endTime, slotDuration 15-480, bufferTime 0-120; no overlapping time blocks within a day. dailyConfigs required on create.

items.title: BR: event/exception owner scoping (mutations owner-only)
items.notes: Event/exception write and detail-read operations require owner === user.id (403 otherwise). listScheduleExceptions uses checkBookingEventOwnership which grants admin/support a bypass. Note GET event/slot reads are intentionally public (discovery) with no owner gate.

items.title: BR: no-show timing windows + exclusivity + confirmed-only
items.notes: markNoShowBooking: booking must be 'confirmed' (INVALID_STATUS_FOR_NO_SHOW); client must wait 5 min, host 10 min past scheduledAt (NO_SHOW_TOO_EARLY); only one party may mark (NO_SHOW_ALREADY_MARKED if already no_show_*). Marker type determines resulting status (client->no_show_host, host->no_show_client).

items.title: BR: slot generation idempotency + timezone correctness + exception filtering
items.notes: Slots stored in UTC via fromZonedTime(owner timezone); generation steps by slotDuration+bufferTime and never exceeds the time block. Bulk insert uses ON CONFLICT (event,startTime) DO NOTHING so re-runs don't duplicate. Regeneration deletes only FUTURE available slots for that event (never booked). Slots overlapping schedule exceptions (incl. recurring occurrences, capped at maxOccurrences/endDate) are filtered out.

items.title: BR: slot must be 'available' to book
items.notes: createBooking throws ConflictError if slot.status !== 'available' (prevents double-booking). On success slot is atomically flipped to 'booked' with the booking ref. Concurrency-sensitive (two clients racing the same slot).

items.title: BR: slot released back to available on cancel/reject/auto-reject
items.notes: On cancel, reject, and timer auto-reject, the booking's slot is set status='available', booking=null so it can be re-booked. Auto-reject does this in the same transaction as the status flip. Invariant: a freed slot must not still reference a terminal booking.

items.title: Show online-booking-unavailable when branch has booking disabled
items.notes: config.enabled === false gate. Branch not accepting online bookings must direct the patient to call instead of presenting a dead form. FE test 'shows a disabled-branch empty state'.

items.title: Show online-booking-unavailable when branch has booking disabled
items.notes: config.enabled === false gate. Branch not accepting online bookings must direct the patient to call instead of presenting a dead form. FE test 'shows a disabled-branch empty state'.

items.title: Recover from slot-taken (409) by bouncing back to the grid
items.notes: On commit failure (409 SLOT_TAKEN — concurrent double-book) the wizard toasts, returns to the slot step, clears the selection, and refetches availability. Double-booking race guard at the patient/calendar boundary — full layers. FE test 'slot-taken on commit bounces back to the slot grid'.

items.title: Accept & Sign disabled until both a stroke and a signer name exist
items.notes: SignaturePad.canSubmit guard: empty signature or empty name keeps the button disabled (no endpoint call). FE-only client gate guarding a clinical/legal submit — no server call → fe-unit only.

items.title: Accept & Sign disabled until both a stroke and a signer name exist
items.notes: SignaturePad.canSubmit guard: empty signature or empty name keeps the button disabled (no endpoint call). FE-only client gate guarding a clinical/legal submit — no server call → fe-unit only.

items.title: Failed accept/reject is surfaced (never silently swallowed)
items.notes: 422 already-decided / FSM conflict / network / 5xx surfaced via decision-error banner; a legal e-sign decision must never be a silent no-op. Guards a money/clinical write path → full layer set.

items.title: Failed accept/reject is surfaced (never silently swallowed)
items.notes: 422 already-decided / FSM conflict / network / 5xx surfaced via decision-error banner; a legal e-sign decision must never be a silent no-op. Guards a money/clinical write path → full layer set.

items.title: Decided presentation shows read-only signed-acceptance record instead of sign controls
items.notes: FIX-002/GAP-1: CasePresentationPanel branches on aggregate.presentation.decision !== null → AcceptedPlanViewer. Read-back of who signed (signerName), decisionAt, rejectionReason + immutable itemized plan. Read-only artifact of a clinical/legal record → full layer set.

items.title: Only treatment-presentation roles can mint a case presentation
items.notes: canPresentCase(role) (rbac.ts) gates whether onPresent is passed to PlanRow; mirrors backend createCasePresentation gate. Non-presentation roles never see the button. Inter-module FE/BE authz boundary → full layer set.

items.title: Only treatment-presentation roles can mint a case presentation
items.notes: canPresentCase(role) (rbac.ts) gates whether onPresent is passed to PlanRow; mirrors backend createCasePresentation gate. Non-presentation roles never see the button. Inter-module FE/BE authz boundary → full layer set.

items.title: Only room admins may start/end video calls
items.notes: room.admins must include caller's Person ID to start a video call (sendChatMessage video_call branch -> 403 'Only room admin can start video calls') and to end one (endVideoCall -> 403 'Only room admin can end video calls'). Join/leave/participant-update do NOT require admin (any participant).

items.title: Leave/update require active in-call membership
items.notes: leaveVideoCall and updateVideoCallParticipant: caller must have a participant entry (404 'not a participant in this video call'), must not have already left (404 'already left'); updateVideoCallParticipant additionally requires joinedAt set (ValidationError 'not joined the video call yet'). Operations only affect the caller's own participant record (self-service, keyed on user.id).

items.title: Room creator must be a participant or admin
items.notes: createChatRoom: authenticated user's Person ID must appear in participants OR admins; otherwise 403 ForbiddenError 'You can only create chat rooms you are involved in'. Prevents creating rooms on behalf of others.

items.title: ICE server list is config-driven
items.notes: getIceServers returns exactly config.webrtc.iceServers (TURN/STUN). No DB lookup, no participant scoping — environment-config surface for WebRTC negotiation.

items.title: Messages immutable; text <= 5000 chars, non-empty
items.notes: ChatMessageRepository.createTextMessage: rejects content > 5000 chars and empty/whitespace-only content (throws); stored trimmed. No update/delete handlers exist for chat_message rows (append-only / immutable). validateMessageContent mirrors the same rule. messageTypeEnum: text | system | video_call. System messages are server-generated only (join/leave/end/mute events).

items.title: Chat room requires >= 2 unique participants
items.notes: createChatRoom: participants array must be non-empty (ValidationError 'At least one participant'), must have >= 2 entries (ValidationError 'two participants required'), and must contain no duplicates (BusinessLogicError DUPLICATE_PARTICIPANTS via Set comparison).

items.title: Cannot join a call already joined
items.notes: joinVideoCall: if an existing participant entry has joinedAt set and no leftAt, 409 ConflictError 'User is already in the video call'. A previously-left participant may rejoin (addVideoCallParticipant updates the existing entry with a fresh joinedAt).

items.title: Room access restricted to participants
items.notes: Cross-cutting authz guard on every room-scoped read/write/WS: room.participants must include caller's Person ID, else 403 ForbiddenError ('not a participant') on HTTP and ws.close(1008 'Not authorized') on the socket. Authorization is by Person ID directly (no profile/role lookups).

items.title: Room lastMessageAt + messageCount maintained on every message
items.notes: ChatRoomRepository.updateLastMessage: reads current room then writes lastMessageAt = message timestamp and messageCount = current + 1 (read-then-write increment, not atomic SQL increment — concurrency note). Invoked on text send, video-call end system message, and WS chat.message persistence. setActiveVideoCall toggles activeVideoCallMessage (set on start, null on end/auto-end).

items.title: At most one active video call per room
items.notes: sendChatMessage video_call branch: findActiveVideoCall (most recent message with status 'starting'|'active') must be null before starting a new call, else 409 ConflictError 'An active video call already exists in this room'. room.activeVideoCallMessage is the single-call reference, set on start and cleared on end/auto-end.

items.title: Video call status FSM (starting -> active -> ended)
items.notes: videoCallStatusEnum: starting | active | ended | cancelled. New call starts 'starting' (createVideoCallMessage forces it). First join transitions 'starting'->'active' and mints roomUrl/token. End (admin) or auto-end (last participant leaves) transitions ->'ended' with endedAt + durationMinutes. updateVideoCallData computes durationMinutes when status->ended and startedAt set. findActiveVideoCall only matches starting/active. Repo guards: target message must be messageType video_call with videoCallData else throws.

items.notes: Money. activePaymentPlans count + paymentPlansBehind from dashboard summary. Amber accent + 'N behind' badge when behind>0. Gated by canViewFinancials; non-financial roles see Checked In count instead.

items.title: PIN-session + auth + org-context guard on dashboard route
items.notes: _dashboard layout beforeLoad: requireAuth, then server-side pinSession check (no/expired/locked → redirect /auth/pin-select), then loadOrgContext (no branch → redirect /dental-onboarding). Patient-safety: protects access to dashboard subtree.

items.title: PIN-session + auth + org-context guard on dashboard route
items.notes: _dashboard layout beforeLoad: requireAuth, then server-side pinSession check (no/expired/locked → redirect /auth/pin-select), then loadOrgContext (no branch → redirect /dental-onboarding). Patient-safety: protects access to dashboard subtree.

items.title: BR: Audit log is append-only at the DB layer (BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raises)
items.notes: V-AUD-IMM-001: migration 0080_audit_log_append_only installs a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE FOR EACH ROW trigger on dental_audit_log that RAISEs 'append-only'. INSERT succeeds; row-level UPDATE/DELETE refused even via direct SQL (compromised credential / errant migration) — defense-in-depth. Reset only via table TRUNCATE. DB-level invariant verified against real Postgres.

items.title: BR: Audit events are append-only at the HTTP layer (405 AUDIT_EVENT_IMMUTABLE)
items.notes: PATCH/PUT/DELETE on /dental/audit-events/:id → 405 with code AUDIT_EVENT_IMMUTABLE regardless of auth state and route param. No mutating handler/route exists. Compliance rule.

items.title: BR: dental_audit_log written first as the authoritative sink, never starved by legacy
items.notes: logAuditEvent writes dental_audit_log FIRST (the spec-compliant table the viewer reads, the authoritative sink) so it is never starved by a failure of the legacy dental_audit write below. Legacy write is SECOND and fire-and-forget (failure logged, swallowed).

items.title: BR: Base PHI-read union is actor-scoped to active branch members (leak-safe)
items.notes: audit_log_entry has no branch/tenant column, so base data-access reads are scoped by ACTOR — only rows whose user is an ACTIVE dental_membership of the viewed branch (the same table the viewer authorizes against). No active members → empty result. Only ids surfaced; base details dropped by caller. Tenancy/PHI guard → full layers.

items.title: BR: Non-enum action filter short-circuits the base-sink query (no 500)
items.notes: audit_log_entry.action is a constrained Postgres ENUM (create/read/update/delete/login/logout). The dental viewer's action filter is freeform (e.g. invoice.voided), so a non-enum value passed to the base query would raise 'invalid input value for enum audit_action' (500). Guarded: a non-base action filter can match no base row → empty result, never a crash.

items.title: BR: branchId is REQUIRED on the audit read (omitted → 400, prevents cross-tenant leak)
items.notes: assertBranchRole(db, user.id, branchId, ['dentist_owner']) checks the dental_membership table (EM-AUD-009 — NOT user.role which onboarding never sets to dentist_owner). A non-owner → 403; a dentist_owner of org A passing org B's branchId has no membership there → 403 (cross-tenant denied). Authz/tenancy guard → full layers.

items.title: BR: Audit date-range validation (unparseable → 400, from>to → 422 INVALID_DATE_RANGE)
items.notes: V-AUD-002: from/to validated up front (before the role DB lookup) for fast input-error feedback. Unparseable date → 400 ValidationError; from after to → 422 BusinessLogicError code INVALID_DATE_RANGE (previously returned an empty set silently, masking a malformed query). Read-path validation.

items.title: BR: Audit-write durability — transient retry, explicit id, fail-closed for security events
items.notes: V-AUD-007/EM-AUD-008 (ADR-005): a transient pooled-connection drop is retried once on a fresh connection (idempotent); the row id is generated explicitly (no reliance on a drifted DB default that made writes fail NOT NULL); a failed dental_audit_log write for a security-class event (or opt-in failClosed) RETHROWS rather than being swallowed, so a money/clinical/security mutation can never report success with no audit row. Non-security stays fire-and-forget.

items.title: BR: Cross-module audit facades expose only PHI-free projections
items.notes: Both read facades expose only the minimal non-PHI shape: findAuditEventsByActions returns action/timestamp/metadata (never snapshots), optionally tenant-scoped, newest-first, empty actions → []; base-phi-reads.facade returns ids + request context only (no base details JSONB). Boundary-exempt .facade files that read foreign schemas but leak no PHI.

items.title: BR: Every audit row carries mandatory fields; actorId = the true session user
items.notes: FIX-004 baseline: after N mixed logAuditEvent calls the two sinks (dental_audit_log vs legacy dental_audit) must hold the SAME rows by count and per action. Because the legacy write is fire-and-forget it can silently drop while the authoritative succeeds; this canary fails loudly the day they diverge (Q3 legacy-sunset gate). Read-only observation; must not change logAuditEvent.

items.title: BR: Merged-sink pagination — fetch top window of each source, merge desc, slice; total = sum
items.notes: #18 single-pane paging: fetch top (offset+limit) of each source (dental + base), merge desc by timestamp, slice the requested window. total = dentalTotal + base.total (each source's unpaginated count). Base sink included only when eventType filter unset or 'data-access'. limit clamped to <=200 (default 50). Read-only paging contract.

items.title: BR: No PHI in the log body — recursive sanitizer at the repository choke point
items.notes: The store is append-only/never-deleted so PHI is unremediable. A recursive case-insensitive blocklist (name/email/dob/ssn/mrn/diagnosis/medications/notes/chiefComplaint/...) strips identity/demographic/clinical keys at any nesting depth (objects + arrays) in metadata + before/after snapshots. Applied in AuditLogRepository.insert so EVERY write path is covered (not only logAuditEvent); idempotent; never throws (drops field rather than leak). Compliance/PHI → full layers.

items.title: BR: Viewing the audit log is itself a self-audited security event
items.notes: V-AUD-NEW-B / WF-028 / AUDIT_CONTRACTS §3: getAuditEvents writes one security-class 'audit_log.accessed' event (actor = viewing owner, target_type = dental_audit_log) capturing filter scope/result counts ONLY — never PHI. Single insert, cannot recurse; logAuditEvent never throws so it cannot break the read. Compliance → full layers.

items.title: BR: Viewer DTO omits before/after snapshot columns
items.notes: V-AUD-003: toDTO deliberately excludes beforeSnapshot/afterSnapshot JSONB from the viewer response (defense-in-depth beyond the write-time sanitizer — they may carry latent PHI). base details JSONB likewise dropped, replaced with {source:'base'}. PHI exposure surface → full layers.

items.title: Fail-closed audit on money mutations
items.notes: P1-C: money/compliance mutations call logAuditEvent with { failClosed: true } so the operation never silently commits without its audit row (P2-A AUD-BR-004 captures before/after + reason on void/discount). Tests: audit-write-reliability.test.ts.

items.title: Insurance claim status FSM (draft→ready→submitted→…→paid/denied/written_off)
items.notes: INSURANCE_CLAIM_FSM: draft→ready→submitted→{under_review,approved,denied}; under_review→{approved,denied}; approved→{partially_paid,paid,denied}; partially_paid→{paid,denied}; denied→{appealed,written_off}; appealed→{submitted,written_off}; paid/written_off terminal. Invalid → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION. Property tests: claim.fsm.property.test.ts.

items.title: Credit apply cap: > 0, <= balance, <= available credit (BR-052)
items.notes: BR-052: appliedCents > 0 (else ValidationError), <= live.balanceCents (EXCEEDS_INVOICE_BALANCE), <= available credit (EXCEEDS_AVAILABLE_CREDIT). No credit → NO_CREDIT; settled balance → INVOICE_SETTLED; voided → INVOICE_VOIDED. Ledger is append-only signed; available = SUM(amountCents) never negative; reads under the per-patient lock so credit can never be over-drawn/double-applied. Tests: dental-billing.patient-credits.test.ts.

items.title: Discount rate bounded 0-100 (money-integrity, INVALID_DISCOUNT_RATE)
items.notes: V-BIL-001: percentageRate must be finite and 0–100 else 422 INVALID_DISCOUNT_RATE. >100 drives totalCents negative; <0 inflates total. Defense-in-depth beyond schema validator.

items.title: Due-date payment-terms precedence (BR-048)
items.notes: resolvePaymentTermsDays: invoice override (paymentTermsDays) → MAX of line-item service terms (getProcedureTermsForCdtCodes, dental-visit) → clinic default (getBranchDefaultPaymentTermsDays, dental-org) → 0. Bounded 0–365. Tests: utils/payment-terms.test.ts, dental-billing.payment-terms.test.ts.

items.title: Cross-tenant report scoping when branchId omitted (EM-BIL-002)
items.notes: EM-BIL-002: every billing report/list with OPTIONAL branchId scopes to the caller's active branches (getActiveBranchIdsForPerson + withTenantTx) when branchId omitted — NEVER the whole multi-tenant DB. Supplied branchId asserts membership. Prevents cross-org invoice/payment/claim/balance + PHI leak. Tests: dental-billing.cross-tenant-reports.test.ts.

items.title: Installment count bounded 2-24 (INVALID_INSTALLMENT_COUNT)
items.notes: V-BIL-002: numberOfInstallments must be integer 2–24 else 422 INVALID_INSTALLMENT_COUNT. Zero/unbounded yields floor(total/0)=Infinity (money-integrity breach). Defense-in-depth beyond schema.

items.title: Invoice needs >=1 billable treatment (NO_BILLABLE_TREATMENTS)
items.notes: S1-T7/WFG-004: getTreatmentsForInvoiceLocked takes a row FOR UPDATE lock; treatments already carrying billedInvoiceId → 422 TREATMENT_ALREADY_BILLED. Concurrent invoice creates for same visit serialize at the lock. Tests: createDentalInvoice.concurrency.test.ts.

items.title: Signed consent required before invoicing (CONSENT_REQUIRED)
items.notes: hasSignedConsentForVisit(tx, visitId) must be true; else 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED. Inter-module guard against dental-clinical consent_form.

items.title: Invoice create authz: dentist_owner/associate only (V-BIL-003)
items.notes: assertBranchRole(['dentist_owner','dentist_associate']) on body.branchId. staff_full NOT permitted to create invoices/plans (V-BIL-003).

items.title: Invoice status FSM (draft → issued → paid/partial/overdue/voided/uncollectible)
items.notes: BR-012 lifecycle invariant enforced across handlers + repo: payments valid only on issued/partial/overdue; draft → INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION; paid/voided → INVOICE_IMMUTABLE. Property tests: invoice.fsm.property.test.ts.

items.title: Invoice create idempotency by localId (offline replay)
items.notes: SL-01/E-NEW-05/GAP-001: a retried create carrying a previously-seen localId echoes the existing invoice (with line items) idempotently, running BEFORE the already-billed guard. Tests: createDentalInvoice.idempotency.test.ts.

items.title: Invoice tax server-derived from branch taxMode (never caller)
items.notes: BR-054/EM-BILL-001 privilege-escalation guard: taxRate/taxCents computed via computeInvoiceTax(getBranchTaxConfig); non_vat → 0, vat_registered → 12% carved OUT of VAT-inclusive gross (total stays = subtotal). Tests: dental-billing.invoice-tax.test.ts, utils/tax.test.ts.

items.title: LOA coverage cap + copay split on claim (BR-057)
items.notes: BR-057: approved/partial LOA caps covered at approvedAmountCents/per-procedure coveredProcedures, bounded by profile annual-limit remaining (estimateCoverage engine). claim.approvedAmountCents=covered, patientPortionCents=billed-covered; per-line approvedAmountCents+status (covered/partial/disallowed). No approved LOA → patient owes full billed. Matches estimateClaimCoverage output.

items.title: HMO LOA validity gate on claim creation (LOA_EXPIRED)
items.notes: BR-056: an authorizationId referencing an LOA with status expired/denied OR validUntil < today (inclusive of validUntil day) → 422 LOA_EXPIRED. 'requested' LOA allowed but applies no coverage (logged warn). LOA must belong to same patient.

items.title: Payment idempotency + cross-invoice receipt reuse guard
items.notes: V-BIL-009/N-BIL-01: receiptNumber doubles as idempotency key SCOPED to this invoice. Same invoice+receipt+amount → 200 replay; same invoice+receipt+diff amount → 409; receipt used on a DIFFERENT invoice → 409 (never echo another invoice's payment; global unique index would 500). findByInvoiceAndReceiptNumber + findByReceiptNumber (global cross-invoice check stays on db).

items.title: Overpayment guard with atomic re-assert (PAYMENT_EXCEEDS_BALANCE)
items.notes: V-BIL-004: amount > balanceCents → 422 PAYMENT_EXCEEDS_BALANCE. amount <= 0 → INVALID_AMOUNT (BR-015). Re-asserted ATOMICALLY at write time via addPayment guardBalance/guardStatus (0 rows → distinguish INVOICE_IMMUTABLE vs PAYMENT_EXCEEDS_BALANCE race).

items.title: Payment plan status FSM (on_track/behind/completed/defaulted)
items.notes: PAYMENT_PLAN_FSM: on_track↔behind, both → completed|defaulted; completed/defaulted terminal (no edges). Property tests: payment-plan.fsm.property.test.ts, dental-billing.payment-plan-fsm.test.ts.

items.title: Patient hasActivePaymentPlan flag sync on plan terminal transition
items.notes: FIX-006: a plan's create / terminal (completed|defaulted) transition recomputes the patient's hasActivePaymentPlan archive flag from a single choke point. Tests: dental-billing.payment-plan-flag-sync.test.ts.

items.title: BIR receipt VAT breakdown + statement (BR-055)
items.notes: BR-055: non-VAT clinic → VAT fields 0, full gross as vatExempt, NON_VAT_STATEMENT; VAT-registered → VATable + 12% VAT broken out, VAT_STATEMENT. Fields/wording only (ATP/CAS e-accreditation out of scope).

items.title: Refund cap: non-void payment/invoice, amount <= un-refunded remainder
items.notes: BR-053: voided payment → PAYMENT_VOIDED; voided invoice → INVOICE_VOIDED; fully refunded → ALREADY_REFUNDED; amount > (payment.amountCents − already-refunded) → EXCEEDS_REFUNDABLE. Re-read under the advisory lock guards a concurrent void (which also reverses the invoice). Multi-refunds capped at remainder; over-cap → 422 no mutation.

items.title: Remittance idempotency on (claim, remittanceReference)
items.notes: Replay on (claimId, remittanceReference): same ref + same amount → 200 replay; same ref + different amount → 409 ConflictError. findByClaimAndReference.

items.title: Remittance over-post guard with advisory lock (PAYER_OVERPOST)
items.notes: Cumulative paidByPayer + disallowed + new must not exceed billedAmountCents → 422 PAYER_OVERPOST. Re-asserted under per-claim pg_advisory_xact_lock (classid 1004) against committed state so concurrent remittances cannot over-post the claim or over-pay the invoice. paid<0 → INVALID_AMOUNT; paid==0 && disallowed==0 → INVALID_AMOUNT.

items.title: RLS P1b branch isolation via withTenantTx (app_rls second wall)
items.notes: All billing payload writes route through withTenantTx([branchIds]) so the app_rls policies on dental_invoice/dental_payment/dental_insurance_claim/dental_payer_payment (Tier-1) + treatment/consent (Tier-2a) enforce branch scope as a second wall and writes stay atomic. Entity-fetch/authz/global cross-tenant checks intentionally stay on db. Tests: dental-billing.rls-activation-invoices-payments/claims-plans/reports.test.ts.

items.title: Void/write-off blocked by active payment plan (ACTIVE_PAYMENT_PLAN)
items.notes: BR-011: an invoice with a plan in on_track|behind cannot be voided → 409/422 ACTIVE_PAYMENT_PLAN. Staff must resolve the plan first.

items.title: BR-003 — no clinical writes to a locked/completed visit
items.notes: Cross-cutting: all five clinical create handlers (prescription, consent, consent-refusal, lab-order, attachment) and non-status prescription edits reject writes when visit.status is locked/completed -> 422 VISIT_IMMUTABLE (attachment uses code VISIT_LOCKED). Carve-out: prescription dispense/cancel and lab-order FSM progression are intentionally exempt (external pharmacy/lab events).

items.title: BR-014 / V-CLN-005 — signed consent form is immutable
items.notes: A signed consent cannot be re-signed (422 CONSENT_FORM_SIGNED) and cannot be revoked (422 CONSENT_ALREADY_SIGNED). No edit, no deletion. signatureData/signedAt frozen at signing.

items.title: BR-017 / EM-CLI-005 — prescriberMemberId must be active dentist membership in branch
items.notes: createPrescription validates prescriberMemberId via getActiveMembershipByIdForClinical(db, prescriberMemberId, visit.branchId). Prevents a member ID from another branch or an inactive member -> 403 ForbiddenError. Cross-module dependency on dental-org membership facade.

items.title: BR-018 — lab order lifecycle FSM (ordered -> in_fabrication -> delivered -> fitted / cancelled)
items.notes: LAB_ORDER_TRANSITIONS forward-only, no skipping; fitted/cancelled terminal; cancel allowed from ordered/in_fabrication/delivered. repo.updateStatus guards -> handler maps to 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION (V-CLN-008). Sets deliveredAt/fittedAt/cancelledAt timestamps.

items.title: V-CLN-011 — amendment originalRecordId must resolve for in-module record types
items.notes: originalRecordResolves validates prescription/consent/labOrder/medicalHistory ids -> 404 on dangling reference; cross-module/free-form types (treatment/note) pass unchecked (cannot assert across bounded contexts). Prevents dangling references on the clinical audit trail.

items.title: FR1.12 / P1-2 — allergy cross-check + drug-drug interaction (non-blocking prescribing warnings)
items.notes: On prescribe: substring-match drugName against the patient's active allergy entries (allergyConflicts) and run checkDrugInteractions against active medication entries (drugInteractions, curated dental-relevant table in utils/drug-interactions.ts). WARN-only (never blocks the write); warnings returned in the 201 response envelope. Patient-safety scope -> full layer coverage.

items.title: Medical history entry is immutable (PATCH not allowed)
items.notes: updateMedicalHistoryEntry.ts. AC-CLI-005/V-CLN-009 clinical safety invariant: medical-history entries are append-only — in-place PATCH always rejected with 405 MEDICAL_HISTORY_IMMUTABLE. Corrections flow through a new entry or the additive amendment path (WF-038). Auth required.

items.title: MODULE_SPEC §6 — medical history & review write roles (dentist/associate/staff_full, no hygienist)
items.notes: V-CLI-002 role guard: medical-history create + review allowed only for dentist_owner/dentist_associate/staff_full; hygienist NOT in the matrix -> ForbiddenError. Branch resolved via patient.preferredBranchId (403 if patient has no assigned branch).

items.title: EM-CLI-012 — prescription status FSM (pending -> dispensed | cancelled, terminal)
items.notes: PRESCRIPTION_TRANSITIONS: pending->[dispensed,cancelled]; dispensed/cancelled terminal. repo.updateStatus guards; handler maps invalid transition to 422 INVALID_PRESCRIPTION_TRANSITION. Generated Zod enum already rejects unknown status values (400).

items.title: V-CLN-010 — a revoked consent can never be signed nor satisfy the treatment/billing gate
items.notes: Enforced in 3 layers: (1) signConsentForm rejects signing a revoked form (422 CONSENT_FORM_REVOKED); (2) clinical-visit.facade.hasSignedConsentForVisit and (3) consent-billing.facade.hasSignedConsentForVisit both require signed=true AND revoked=false, so a revoked consent never satisfies the BR-014 treatment gate or the billing consent gate.

items.title: Erasure ANONYMIZES PII in place, never hard-deletes the record
items.notes: V-DG-002-AN. erasure-engine safety invariant 2: targets redact PII (name→[ERASED] pseudonym, contactInfo/identifiers nulled) and KEEP the de-identified clinical/billing row. The ONLY hard delete is the physical S3 radiograph object + its storage file row. Visit/treatment/prescription/invoice need no target (covered by Person+Patient anonymization).

items.title: Per-subject advisory lock serializes approve / reject / placeLegalHold
items.notes: WFG-006 hold-vs-erasure race. approve and reject each run pg_advisory_xact_lock(1003, hashtext(subjectPersonId)) — the same ns 1003 lock placeLegalHold takes — and keep the hold-read + status transition + anonymize inside ONE locked tx. Guarantees a concurrent hold either commits before the read (we see it → block) or cannot commit until we finish; prevents the approve+reject clobber and double-anonymize. Idempotent transitionFromRequested is the conditional-WHERE guard.

items.title: Anonymize engine is DRY-RUN by default (explicit opt-in to act)
items.notes: erasure-engine safety invariant 1: anonymizeSubject defaults dryRun=true and only mutates when dryRun:false. Approval passes dryRun:false (the opt-in); dry-run reports intent, touches nothing, still audits erasure.dry_run. Hard invariant lives in the engine so no caller path can accidentally anonymize.

items.title: Approve/reject only valid from status='requested' (FSM guard)
items.notes: Lifecycle requested → anonymized | rejected (both terminal). Pre-write status check + repo.transitionFromRequested's conditional WHERE status='requested' both reject any approve/reject on a non-requested row → ValidationError "Cannot approve/reject an erasure request in status '<status>'". NotFound when the request id is missing.

items.title: Anonymization is idempotent (re-run anonymizes 0 → noop, still audited)
items.notes: erasure-engine: a re-run over an already-anonymized subject acts on 0 rows → outcome 'noop' (still audited). fileIdsPendingS3Delete is recomputed from current rows each run so a retry re-deletes still-pending S3 objects safely. The transitionFromRequested guard means a second approve matches 0 rows → anonymize runs at most once per request.

items.title: Active legal hold blocks erasure (headline invariant)
items.notes: V-DG-002-LH. approveErasure consults the real dental_legal_hold store (isPersonUnderLegalHold) OR a reviewer-asserted legalHold flag; on a hold the request is marked `rejected` with legalHoldBlocked=true + rejectionReason 'Subject under an active legal hold', NO subject data touched, engine short-circuits before any target (audited erasure.blocked_legal_hold). Never bypassable; releasing the hold lets a fresh request anonymize.

items.title: Erasure never touches the audit trail; the audit record outlives the erased identity
items.notes: V-DG-002-AU. erasure-engine safety invariants 3+5: audit is not a target; the engine only APPENDS its own events (erasure.requested / dry_run / anonymized / blocked_legal_hold / rejected / s3_deleted) — it has NO purge/modify path. erasure.anonymized is a `security` event so an audit-write failure fails the operation closed. dental_audit_log has no FK to person, so the erasure audit row survives anonymization of the subject.

items.title: All erasure endpoints are platform-admin-only (non-admin → 403)
items.notes: EM-DG-RBAC: every handler requires user.role==='admin' (Better-Auth admin plugin); unauthenticated → 401, non-admin → 403 ForbiddenError. Admin scope is platform-level / cross-tenant BY DESIGN (DPO function), tenantId/subjectPersonId come from the request, not per-branch membership.

items.title: Erasure subject must be a valid, owned patient (V1 patients-only)
items.notes: Three guards in requestErasure: (a) a supplied subjectPatientId that resolves to no patient → ValidationError (forged claim, no silent fallback); (b) C-4 person-only subjects (no patient anchor) are out of V1 erasure scope → ValidationError (would keep caller tenantId = cross-tenant leak); (c) patient not owned by the named person (subjectPatient.personId !== subjectPersonId) → ValidationError.

items.title: Tenancy derived from the subject, not the caller body (anti-forgery)
items.notes: FIX-001 (GAP-2), EM-BIL-002 class. requestErasure resolves branch→organization from the subject's patient row (getErasureSubjectPatient + getBranchOrganizationId) and REJECTS a forged/mismatched caller-supplied tenantId or branchId. When fully resolvable the server owns tenancy; when unresolvable (orphan branch) it keeps supplied values.

items.title: CBCT finalize clean-fails on malformed DICOM (no half-written state)
items.notes: Bad base64 or DicomParseError → 422 INVALID_DICOM with NO write to the image row (volume columns stay at pre-finalize state). image must belong to studyId else 404. Clinical-safety atomicity.

items.title: AI landmarks must enter at 'placed' — no AI auto-confirm/lock
items.notes: source ai/ai_corrected with status confirmed/locked → 422 AI_LANDMARK_CANNOT_AUTOCONFIRM. detect always writes source='ai' status='placed'. Human-in-the-loop safety (plan §4).

items.title: BR-041/CIMG-007: missing/non-member ceph image → 404 (never 403)
items.notes: Any ceph op on a non-existent image OR by a non-member → 404 (anti-enumeration), never 403-for-missing. All ceph routes are image-scoped (no study-level ceph endpoint).

items.title: CIMG-003: landmark FSM strictly forward placed→confirmed→locked
items.notes: CEPH_LANDMARK_TRANSITIONS: locked is terminal; backward/invalid transition → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION (SM-02).

items.title: CIMG-004/CIMG-014: locked landmark fully immutable (3 axes)
items.notes: Coordinate PATCH / status PATCH / DELETE on a locked landmark → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED. Batch upsert silently skips locked entries (no partial application).

items.title: BR-043/CIMG-010: steiner_hybrid_sn mandatory analysis label (v1.4)
items.notes: analysisType validated against ANALYSIS_TYPES; unknown → 422 UNSUPPORTED_ANALYSIS_TYPE; steiner_hybrid_sn is the default/canonical v1.4 label.

items.title: BR-038/CIMG-015: recompute idempotent, never writes a report
items.notes: Writes imagingCephAnalyses only; identical output for unchanged landmark state; returns single CephAnalysis (not {items,analysis}). D-I immutability of report rows preserved.

items.title: Recompute preconditions: INSUFFICIENT_LANDMARKS / NOT_CALIBRATED
items.notes: Per-protocol reference landmark set required (else 422 INSUFFICIENT_LANDMARKS); uncalibrated/null pixelSpacing → 422 NOT_CALIBRATED. Must never return a silent all-null 200 (V-IMG-004 clinical safety).

items.title: BR-039: calibration provenance frozen in report snapshot
items.notes: pixelSpacingMm + pinned versioned calibration record (points/distance/version) frozen into snapshot.calibration at creation; later recalibration does not retroactively change existing snapshots. Missing-landmark measurements are null (not errors).

items.title: Per-image advisory lock serializes report finalize + revision lineage
items.notes: pg_advisory_xact_lock(4001, hashtext(imageId)) inside the finalize tx makes the loser re-read committed vN+1 so revisionOf points at it (no forked lineage on concurrent finalize). Concurrency invariant.

items.title: CIMG-006: report gated on A,B,Go,Po all confirmed
items.notes: CEPH_REPORT_GATE_LANDMARKS A/B/Go/Po must be confirmed or locked; otherwise 422 REPORT_GATE_UNCONFIRMED. Clinical safety gate.

items.title: CIMG-008/CIMG-012/BR-042: reports are immutable monotonic versions
items.notes: No update/delete handler exists. version monotonically increasing per image; DB unique(imageId,version) prevents duplicates/gaps and makes concurrent create race-safe.

items.title: G4-B sign-off split: assistants draft only, dentist confirms/locks/finalizes
items.notes: isCephDraftRole → may place/edit drafts; non-draft/non-member → 404. Transition into confirmed/locked or report finalize requires isCephSignoffRole else 403 ASSISTANT_CANNOT_FINALIZE. Report records prepared_by vs finalized_by provenance.

items.title: Superimposition v1 honesty: cranial-base only + same-patient + calibration gate
items.notes: Non-v1 reference → 422 SUPERIMPOSITION_NOT_IMPLEMENTED; cross-patient → 422 SUPERIMPOSITION_PATIENT_MISMATCH; insufficient → 422 INSUFFICIENT_LANDMARKS; coincident → 422 DEGENERATE_REGISTRATION; mm deltas gated on both timepoints' calibration; mandatory disclosure label (measurement of change, not diagnosis). Clinical interpretation safety.

items.title: CIMG-001/CIMG-002: ceph & CBCT require strict addon tier
items.notes: Strict-equality imagingTier !== 'addon' → 403 IMAGING_TIER_REQUIRED. BOTH 'free' AND 'basic' blocked; null/absent tier = no-access. Gated at study CREATE for cephalometric/cbct, not just downstream. Enforced before any DB write.

items.title: CIMG-009/BR-046: landmark writes trigger inline analysis recompute
items.notes: batchUpsert & updateCephLandmark recompute analysis inline and return {items, analysis} so the response is always fresh after a write.

items.title: BR-027: associate may delete only images they acquired
items.notes: dentist_associate with study.acquiredBy !== user.id → 403; dentist_owner may delete any image in branch.

items.title: BR-030: branch isolation across all imaging endpoints
items.notes: Every handler enforces assertBranchAccess/assertBranchRole on the resolved study.branchId; finding/image details hidden from non-members (404/403). Tenancy invariant.

items.title: G6: server derives pixelSpacingMm from ruler (client value ignored)
items.notes: pixelSpacingMm must be positive (else 422). 2-point ruler is all-or-nothing (partial body → 422); coincident points → 422; authoritative mm/px = knownDistanceMm/pixelDistance computed server-side. Versioned calibration record persisted; image scalar mirrors derived value. Measurement integrity.

items.title: BR-026: image delete default-deny (dentist/associate only)
items.notes: Only dentist_owner/dentist_associate may delete; hygienist/front_desk/all others → 403.

items.title: BR-028: image deletion is soft-only (status='archived')
items.notes: archiveImage() sets status='archived'; no row physically removed.

items.title: Auto-detect kill-switch + cephalometric-only modality gate
items.notes: Feature flag dentalImagingAutoLandmark OFF → 403 FEATURE_DISABLED (deploy-free kill-switch). Non-cephalometric image modality → 422 INVALID_MODALITY. Provider failure surfaces as status='failed' result, never a 500.

items.title: G5b: image links are loose-coupled UUID refs, idempotent
items.notes: linkType in {treatment_plan,ortho_case,report}; targetId UUID-validated; no DB FK to other modules; linking the same (image,type,target) twice returns the existing link (idempotent).

items.title: BR-025: image must be linked to a patient (visit/teeth optional)
items.notes: patient_id NOT NULL on study; visitId + toothNumbers optional join links. Image row persisted immediately so it is queryable post-upload.

items.title: BR-035: legacy attachments surfaced via union adapter
items.notes: dental_attachment xray/photo/scan rows mapped to imaging items (source='legacy'); document + deleted attachments excluded; legacy default diagnostic/ok/untagged, no presigned URL.

items.title: Measurement types require imaging tier != free (annotations free)
items.notes: distance/angle/area on a 'free'-tier branch → 403 (T-08-03). label/arrow/freehand/shape/tooth annotations unrestricted (D3).

items.title: BR-034: upload MIME allowlist (JPEG/PNG/TIFF/BMP + DICOM)
items.notes: Unsupported mimeType → 422 UNSUPPORTED_MIME_TYPE, checked before any other work (ALLOWED_IMAGING_MIME_TYPES).

items.title: BR-032: modality non-nullable default 'other'
items.notes: modality defaults to 'other' on both study and image records when omitted (schema default).

items.title: BR-023: annotations are non-destructive JSON overlays
items.notes: Geometry stored in imaging_annotation.data JSONB; underlying image record never mutated/burned-in.

items.title: BR-033: per-class upload size ceiling (image 100MB / DICOM higher)
items.notes: Oversized payload rejected BEFORE issuing upload URL → 422 FILE_TOO_LARGE (maxUploadSizeForMime clamps DICOM CBCT to hard cap).

items.title: Every place/release transition writes a compliance audit event
items.notes: logAuditEvent on place (legal_hold.placed) and release (legal_hold.released): eventType 'compliance', resourceType 'legal_hold', resourceId=hold.id, personId=actor, metadata carries subjectPersonId (+reason on place). Place audits within the same tx (atomic with the row); release audits on db after update. Subject/actor refs are bare UUIDs (no DB FK, mirrors audit/erasure decoupling).

items.title: Place-hold serializes against erasure approval via per-subject advisory lock
items.notes: pg_advisory_xact_lock(1003, hashtext(subjectPersonId)) taken inside the place transaction (same ns 1003 used by approveErasure/rejectErasure). A hold placed during a mid-flight erasure approval either commits before the approval's hold-read (blocks the erasure) or waits until the approval releases the lock — it can never slip into the window between the hold-read and the irreversible anonymize. Concurrency invariant; inter-module governance race => full layer set.

items.title: Place-hold input validation (tenantId, subjectPersonId, name, reason required)
items.notes: PlaceLegalHoldBody zod: tenantId uuid, subjectPersonId uuid, branchId optional uuid, name min(1) ('name is required'), reason min(1) ('reason is required'), note optional. Missing/invalid => 400 (zValidator). Tested: 'missing name => 400'.

items.title: All legal-hold endpoints are platform-admin-only (non-admin => 403)
items.notes: Every handler requires authenticated user (no user.id => UnauthorizedError 401) AND user.role==='admin' (else ForbiddenError 403). Admin scope is platform-level / cross-tenant BY DESIGN for the DPO/data-governance function — tenantId & subjectPersonId come from the request body/params, not per-branch membership. Tested at legal-hold-routes.test.ts (non-admin forbidden 403).

items.title: Release FSM: active->released is terminal; re-releasing rejected
items.notes: releaseLegalHold guards hold.status !== 'active' => ValidationError 'Legal hold is already {status}'. Prevents silently re-stamping releasedBy/releasedAt or resurrecting a released hold (illegal FSM transition). Tested AC-LH-004.

items.title: EF-ORG-P020: only an active membership grants role privileges
items.notes: getMemberRole / role checks filter on status='active'; a revoked/inactive/invited member must not retain role-based privileges. Applied in branchSettings, consentTemplates, getBranchesByUser, getOrgContext, fee schedule, etc.

items.title: BR-016: branch membership required for all branch-scoped access
items.notes: assertBranchAccess verifies the caller holds an ACTIVE dental_membership for the requested branch; non-member → 403. Foundational tenant/branch isolation across every branch-scoped handler. Source assert-branch-access.ts; test dental-org-auth-p0.test.ts.

items.title: Credential fields never exposed (pinHash/securityAnswerHash/securityQuestion)
items.notes: G7-S2: pinHash, securityAnswerHash, securityQuestion are stripped from every membership response (create/list/deactivate/set-pin/update). Defense against credential-material disclosure.

items.title: Fee resolution precedence: branch override → catalog default → 0
items.notes: resolveFeeCents: a per-branch override (settings.feeSchedule[cdt]) wins; else the global catalog default (dental_procedure_code.default_fee_php); else 0. Shared by getFeeSchedule and createDentalTreatment pricing (closes AC-ORG-002). test fee-resolution.test.ts.

items.title: Onboarding abuse guardrails (per-IP rate limit + verified email in prod)
items.notes: Production-only: per-IP fixed-window rate limit (5/hour, single-instance STOPGAP) → 429 RateLimitError with retryAfter; verified email REQUIRED (its own check, decoupled from sign-in requireEmailVerification) → 403 EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED. Relaxed in dev/test.

items.title: Self-service onboarding restricted to solo/clinic tiers
items.notes: Only solo and clinic tiers self-onboard; group/enterprise → 403 TIER_NOT_SELF_SERVICE (provisioned by sales/admin).

items.title: One active self-service org per owner (app pre-check + DB partial-unique index)
items.notes: App pre-check (existing active org → 409 ORG_LIMIT_REACHED) plus the race-safe backstop: the dental_org_one_active_per_owner partial-unique index; a concurrent create that loses the race (23505 on that constraint) maps to the same friendly 409.

items.title: PHI go-live gate: provisional org blocks patient/visit writes until activated
items.notes: Onboarding leaves org status='provisional'. assert-org-live (shared) gates PHI creation (createDentalPatient, createDentalVisit) on status='live' in production; activateOrganization is the owner's terms/BAA acceptance that flips it. suspended is an ops-only state (not self-recoverable).

items.title: Owner can never revoke its own staff.manage permission
items.notes: updatePermissions refuses an override setting dentist_owner staff.manage=false (400) so an org can never lock itself out of permission/staff administration.

items.title: Permission overrides validated against the feature catalog
items.notes: Unknown feature key → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR. Catalog (PERMISSION_FEATURES) is the single source of truth; defaults mirror current assertBranchRole sets so absent overrides are backward-safe (additive model).

items.title: BR-016b: PIN-based local-auth lockout escalation
items.notes: recordFailedPinAttempt: 30s lockout at the lower threshold escalating to a 5-minute lockout at higher failure counts; locked-out caller → 429 with lockedUntil; a successful verify (or owner reset) clears the counter. Lockout counter is SHARED by verify-pin and recover-pin. Source membership.repo.ts isLockedOut/recordFailedPinAttempt/resetPinAttempts.

items.title: FR6.3: tier-based active-member limit
items.notes: Active-member cap per org tier: solo 2, clinic 5, group 20, enterprise unlimited. Deactivated members do NOT count. At/over limit → 409 TIER_LIMIT_REACHED. Source createMember.ts / DentalMembershipManagement_create.ts; test memberTierLimits.test.ts.

items.title: Tier-limit concurrency: advisory lock + re-count under tx
items.notes: FR6.3 race fix: countActiveStaffByBranch→createOne is a check-then-act with no row to lock (PIN-only staff have personId=null so no unique index bounds it). createMember serializes per branch with pg_advisory_xact_lock(3001, hashtext(branchId)) and re-counts inside the tx so the losing concurrent writer sees the winner's committed member and is rejected — preventing cap overshoot. (db.transaction; membership is not RLS-activated.) test createMember.concurrency.test.ts.

items.title: BR-015b: archived patient is read-only (EF-PAT-001)
items.notes: Every write on an archived patient returns 403 PATIENT_ARCHIVED (update demographics, follow-up note, recall, task, contact, alert, insurance, claim, auth, plan, consent, household add/remove). Uniform EF-PAT-001 guard across the module.

items.title: BR-016: branch membership required for all PHI access
items.notes: assertBranchAccess/assertBranchRole/assertPatientBranchAccess on every handler → 403. V-PAT-002: branch is derived from the patient record and ALWAYS asserted; a missing/branchless patient DENIES (never bypasses). List/export/sync-logs require branchId (400) to prevent cross-branch leaks; conditions/visits never gate auth on the optional branchId filter.

items.title: Claim draft FSM
items.notes: CLAIM_DRAFT_FSM draft→ready→submitted→accepted/rejected; rejected→draft; accepted terminal → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION. submitted stamps submittedAt. Fail-closed audit on transition.

items.title: BR-015: registration requires explicit consent
items.notes: consentGiven must be true else 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED. Single-consent model (V-PAT-004); the captured registration consent is persisted on person.consent.registrationConsent.

items.title: Coverage authorization (LOA) FSM
items.notes: COVERAGE_AUTH_FSM requested→approved/denied; approved→partial/expired; partial→expired; denied/expired terminal → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION. approved/partial may set approvedAmountCents + coveredProcedures (caps the LOA coverage used at claim filing, BR-057) and stamp approvedAt.

items.title: BR-015c: follow-up notes are append-only
items.notes: Only GET (list) and POST (add) routes exist — no PATCH/DELETE — so notes cannot be edited or deleted. JSONB array on the patient record.

items.title: Household invariants (at-most-one + guarantor protection)
items.notes: A patient belongs to at most one household (ALREADY_IN_HOUSEHOLD). Guarantor + members must be in the household branch (GUARANTOR/MEMBER_BRANCH_MISMATCH). Guarantor cannot be removed (GUARANTOR_NOT_REMOVABLE). Archived patients cannot be guarantor/added/removed (PATIENT_ARCHIVED).

items.title: TP-BR-006: server owns plan total (money invariant)
items.notes: Once items are linked, totalEstimateCents is derived from item prices (recomputeTotal) overriding any caller-supplied value — no-op for itemless drafts (manual ballpark preserved). Keeps plans-sheet estimate in lock-step with case-presentation grandTotalCents at all three link callers (present/approve/accept).

items.title: BR-020: patient merge/unmerge deferred (501)
items.notes: Both merge endpoints are admin-only and return a clean 501 (Phase 2). detectDuplicatePatients implements only the detection half. Cross-module cascade undefined (WFG-007).

items.title: EF-PAT-005: PHI reads are audited
items.notes: Sensitive PHI reads write an audit row (patient.view / safety_floor.read / statement.read / treatment_plans.read / insurance_profiles.read / claims.read / recalls.read / tasks.read / contacts.read / alerts.read / sync_logs.read). Keys not values; logger redacts JSONB PHI at any depth.

items.title: Case-presentation decision is terminal (PRESENTATION_DECIDED)
items.notes: A decided presentation cannot be re-decided — pre-check 422 PRESENTATION_DECIDED plus a race-safe second check on the atomic decide() (lost-race → 422). Mirrors V-CLN-005 consent e-sig immutability.

items.title: Provisional org PHI gate (assertOrgLive)
items.notes: C-1: a provisional (not-yet-activated) clinic cannot accumulate PHI — assertOrgLive blocks patient registration until the org is live (production-only).

items.title: Recall FSM + recurrence seeding
items.notes: RECALL_FSM pending→sent/cancelled; sent→completed/cancelled; completed/cancelled terminal → 422 RECALL_INVALID_TRANSITION. Completing a recurring recall (intervalMonths>0) seeds the next-cycle pending recall (D4: trigger on recall-completed, no cross-module visit coupling).

items.title: Sensitive clinical/billing writes are fail-closed audited
items.notes: Plan approval, case-presentation accept, and claim-status change write audit rows with before/after status, fail-closed (the action cannot silently commit without its trail). tenantId resolved to org via getBranchOrgId.

items.title: Sync-log FSM + optimistic-lock conflict
items.notes: SYNC_FSM pending→syncing/failed; syncing→synced/failed; failed→syncing; synced terminal → 422 SYNC_INVALID_TRANSITION. LF-BR-004 stale-write: version mismatch → 409 CONFLICT with conflictPayload. Branchless legacy row immutable (BRANCH_ACCESS_DENIED).

items.title: Patient task FSM
items.notes: TASK_FSM open→in_progress/cancelled; in_progress→done/cancelled; done/cancelled terminal → 422 TASK_INVALID_TRANSITION. done stamps completedAt.

items.title: TP-BR-005 / plan FSM: derived completion + transitions
items.notes: TREATMENT_PLAN_FSM: draft→presented/cancelled; presented→approved/rejected/cancelled; approved→scheduled/partially_completed/cancelled; scheduled→partially_completed/cancelled; partially_completed→completed/cancelled; rejected/completed/cancelled terminal. TP-BR-005: completing one item must NOT complete the whole plan unless ALL items complete (status derived from linked treatments, recomputeStatus). TP-BR-006: denormalized totalEstimateCents server-recomputed from linked item prices on every link point.

items.title: 2017 AAP/EFP staging/grading/extent classification on completion
items.notes: classifyChart/classifyPerio: stage from worst interdental CAL (mesial/distal sites only) + complexity factors that only raise stage (PD>=6, furcation>=2, tooth-loss, mobility>=2, biteCollapse, <20 teeth); grade defaults B, shifted by 5yr progression OR %bone-loss/age ratio + smoking/diabetes modifiers (raise-only); extent localized/generalized/molar_incisor. Stage/extent null without evidence.

items.title: Completed/locked chart rejects further writes (409 CHART_COMPLETED)
items.notes: N-PER-01/V-PER-001/V-PER-002: a non-draft chart is a state conflict (409 CHART_COMPLETED), not a 422 business-rule failure. upsert rejects status!=draft; complete rejects status completed|locked.

items.title: Clinical PHI audit trail on create/complete/lock
items.notes: V-PER-005/006: logAuditEvent writes dental_audit_log rows for perio.chart.created, perio.chart.completed (with summary + classification metadata), perio.chart.locked. Per ADR-006 these domain events are audit-log-only semantic markers.

items.title: Completion summary stats (BoP%, mean depth, deep-pocket count)
items.notes: Aggregates 6 depth + 6 BOP sites per reading: meanDepth=sum/count, bopPercent=true/total*100, deepPocketCount=sites with depth>=5mm. Persisted as numeric (toFixed 2), coerced back to numbers on read.

items.title: Per-site gingival margin must be integer -5..20mm
items.notes: P1-5 assertValidGingivalMargins: 6 GM sites integer -5..20 => else 422 INVALID_GINGIVAL_MARGIN. Signed (apical=recession / coronal=overgrowth); feeds derived CAL.

items.title: Mobility/furcation grades must be 0-3
items.notes: V-PER-004 assertValidGrades: mobility/furcation integers 0-3 => else 422 INVALID_GRADE (Miller mobility / Hamp furcation classes).

items.title: BR-P01 one perio chart per visit
items.notes: findByVisitId before create; duplicate visitId => 409 CHART_EXISTS (ConflictError).

items.title: BR-P02 chart immutable once visit completed/locked
items.notes: Sealed (completed/locked/discarded) visit blocks writes: create => 422 VISIT_LOCKED, upsert => 422 VISIT_IMMUTABLE, complete => 422 VISIT_LOCKED. Visit state read via getVisitForPerio facade.

items.title: BR-P03 probing depths 0-20mm, recession -5..20mm
items.notes: assertValidDepths: each of 6 depth sites integer 0-20, recession integer -5..20 => 422 INVALID_DEPTH (defensive wall behind generated zod 400).

items.title: BR-P04 FDI tooth number must be a valid quadrant range
items.notes: isValidFdiToothNumber: adult 11-18/21-28/31-38/41-48 or primary 51-55/61-65/71-75/81-85. Disjoint-gap (e.g. 19) => 422 INVALID_TOOTH_NUMBER.

items.title: BR-P05 clinical role gate (write vs read scopes)
items.notes: assertBranchRole: writes require dentist_owner|dentist_associate|hygienist; reads additionally allow staff_full. staff_scheduling excluded from all perio (EF-PER-002); non-members => 403.

items.title: BR-P06 tooth-reading upsert idempotent on (chartId, toothNumber)
items.notes: Single atomic onConflictDoUpdate writing ONLY columns present in the request (PATCHABLE_READING_COLUMNS); other sites never reset to null; createdBy/createdAt preserved; no 409 on duplicate; race-free chairside per-keystroke patches.

items.title: BR-P07 minimum readings to complete (16 adult / 8 primary)
items.notes: Dentition inferred from charted FDI numbers: all-primary (51-85) => min 8, else adult min 16. Below minimum => 422 INSUFFICIENT_READINGS.

items.title: Chart patientId must match the visit's patient
items.notes: P1-6 cross-patient create-linkage guard: body.patientId != visit.patientId => 422 PATIENT_VISIT_MISMATCH (prevents mislabeling clinical PHI to a foreign patient; mirrors generatePMD).

items.title: Per-site CAL derived read-only from PD + gingival margin
items.notes: P1-5 computeSiteCal: CAL = PD + GM (signed), clamped at 0; null when either input missing. Never stored, always derived so it cannot drift; attached on every read path and on upsert response.

items.title: V-PER-007 visit-lock to chart-lock cascade (lazy on read/write)
items.notes: cascadeChartLockFromVisit: when parent visit completed/locked and chart not yet locked, lockByVisitId transitions chart to terminal locked + writes perio.chart.locked audit marker (ADR-006 audit-log-only, no event bus). Idempotent under concurrency; reconciled on next perio access.

items.title: BR-021: PMD generated only from completed/locked visit; immutable checksum-sealed snapshot
items.notes: Partial unique index pmd_document_visit_generated_unique on (visit_id,status) WHERE status='generated' makes exactly-one-live-PMD a DB invariant. supersede() marks the old row 'superseded' FIRST then inserts the replacement (order matters under the index). A concurrent visit-completion that trips 23505 is resolved idempotently in generatePmdForVisit (re-reads + returns the winner) rather than surfacing a 500.

items.title: EF-PMD-001: import checksum verification rejects tampered content
items.notes: When body.checksum is supplied, the server recomputes sha256-<hex> of body.content and rejects a mismatch with 422 CHECKSUM_MISMATCH. When absent, import proceeds without verification. Data-integrity on PHI ingestion -> all four layers.

items.title: EF-PMD-003: safety-floor merge is append-only and merge-once (409 on re-merge)
items.notes: Idempotency: fast path returns 409 SAFETY_FLOOR_ALREADY_MERGED when safetyFloorMerged flag is 'true'; the durable backstop is a unique index on imported_pmd_id in imported_pmd_safety_floor_events (23505 -> 409) guarding concurrent double-merge. Items are inserted into med-history FIRST (losing a penicillin allergy is worse than a rare duplicate row) before the event is recorded and the flag set. A merge with no parseable items succeeds as a no-op review (count 0, flag set). Patient-safety -> all four layers.

items.title: EF-PMD-005 / V-PMD-010: sourceDescription required and <=200 chars on import
items.notes: sourceDescription is REQUIRED (identifies originating software e.g. 'Open Dental v21.1') for data-provenance audit, and length-capped at 200 chars (>200 -> 422 VALIDATION_ERROR, guarded in-handler pending central validator regen). Persisted and recorded in the pmd.import audit metadata.

items.title: N-PMD-02: PMD patientId derived from the visit, never from request body
items.notes: A body.patientId disagreeing with visit.patientId -> 422 PATIENT_VISIT_MISMATCH. The sealed snapshot uses visit.patientId exclusively so the request body cannot bind an arbitrary patient into a checksum-sealed record (same single-source-of-truth rule as branch_id). Patient-safety -> all four layers.

items.title: PMD attesting author = visit's treating dentist (not the trigger actor)
items.notes: authorMemberId = visit.dentistMemberId (the treating clinician), not whoever triggered completion. authorMemberId is included in the snapshot BEFORE hashing so the checksum binds the attesting clinician to the content (EF-PMD-004).

items.title: Branch RBAC: generation restricted to clinicians; import/merge to clinicians+staff_full
items.notes: generatePMD requires role dentist_owner|dentist_associate (assertBranchRole); staff_full and non-members -> 403 (N-PMD deny). importPMD / mergeSafetyFloor / exportPMD allow dentist_owner|dentist_associate|staff_full. generatePmdForVisit core additionally checks the actor has an active membership at the visit's branch (else 403). Reads (get/list) use assertBranchAccess (any active member). Authz -> all four layers.

items.title: Cross-branch/tenant isolation on import (no PHI leak across orgs)
items.notes: SL-08 / F-G06: a foreign-org caller cannot import a PMD for another org's patient -> 403 with no row written; the patient-owning org can (201); unauthenticated -> 401. Authorization is anchored to patient.preferredBranchId. Tenancy/PHI -> all four layers.

items.title: Imported PMD is read-only — mutating verbs rejected at the router
items.notes: BR-022 / AC-PMD-002. app.ts registers PATCH/PUT/DELETE /dental/pmd/imported/:id -> 405 IMPORTED_PMD_IMMUTABLE before the generated routes. Imported originals are stored verbatim and never editable/deletable; their safety data is copied forward via merge-safety-floor instead.

items.title: V-PMD-002: cross-module refs stored as plain UUIDs with no DB foreign key
items.notes: visit_id/patient_id/author_member_id/branch_id on pmd_document and patient_id on imported_pmd are plain UUIDs with NO FK, so imports/generation from a defunct facility (or after patient anonymisation/erasure) are not blocked by a missing FK target. Only supersedes_id (intra-aggregate self-ref) keeps its FK.

items.title: V-PMD-007: every PMD PHI movement writes a synchronous audit row
items.notes: import->pmd.import, generation->pmd.generate + pmd.generated (DE-017, satisfies the PMDGenerated domain event via an audit row per ADR-006 no-event-bus), export->pmd.export, merge->pmd.safety_floor.merged. tenantId resolved via getBranchOrgId(branch).organizationId. Import audit is wrapped so it never breaks the ingestion. Compliance/PHI -> all four layers.

items.title: V-PMD-008 / EF-PMD-007: patient-self read/list/export of own PMDs (HIPAA right-of-access)
items.notes: A user whose id === patient.person may read/list/export/download their OWN PMDs and care-record, bypassing the staff branch-role check; everyone else must pass branch authorization. PHI access policy -> all four layers.

items.title: IDOR-free self-scope: every portal read returns ONLY caller's own rows
items.notes: V-PORTAL-001 headline invariant. patientId derived server-side (resolveSelfPatientIdOrThrow → patients.person===user.id); no route accepts client-supplied patientId. A tampered ?patientId=<other> query param is INERT — handler ignores all client input. Patient A can never read B's appointments/invoices/balance; assertSelfPatient: other patient → 403 no leak, nonexistent → 404. Patient-safety/trust-boundary → full layer set.

items.title: Staff-only account denied (403); unauthenticated denied (401)
items.notes: V-PORTAL-002 patient-only boundary. authMiddleware({roles:['user']}) gates the routes; the staff-vs-patient distinction is enforced in-handler (caller with no linked dental_patient row → 403 NOT_A_SELF_PATIENT, never another patient's data). Unauthenticated → 401. Authz/trust-boundary → full layer set.

items.title: Empty self-scope returns [] / zeroed roll-up, never a fallback to all
items.notes: V-PORTAL-003. A real owned patient with zero rows gets [] (appointments/invoices) and a zeroed balance roll-up (0/0/0, invoiceCount 0) — NOT an error and NOT a fallback to another patient's or all data. Facades scope strictly by eq(patientId). Proves the scope is exactly the session patient's set even when empty.

items.title: Patient-appropriate projection; internal fields & written-off/voided debt hidden
items.notes: V-PORTAL-004.
