Projections expose only schedule/billing-facing fields; internal staff fields (dentistMemberId, notes, cancellationReason, discountReason, discountedBy, line items, uncollectibleAt) NOT returned. Voided AND uncollectible (internally written-off) invoices hidden from /me/invoices AND excluded from /me/balance roll-up — a patient is never shown debt the clinic cancelled or wrote off (balance would be 17000/13000 if uncollectible leaked). Money/projection invariant → full layer set.

items.title: Portal is read-only: no /me write/pay/mutate route exists
items.notes: V-PORTAL-005. No POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on any /me route — generated route table registers ONLY GET /me/appointments|/me/invoices|/me/balance. A patient cannot mutate clinic-owned data (mark treatment performed, edit notes, void/pay invoice) through the portal. Self-booking and self-pay are Phase-2 deferred; their absence IS the guarantee. Asserted both via live write-verb requests (404/405, never 2xx) and a source-of-truth grep over routes.ts.

items.title: Appointment status FSM (APPOINTMENT_TRANSITIONS)
items.notes: scheduled→{confirmed,checked_in,cancelled,no_show}; confirmed→{checked_in,cancelled,no_show}; checked_in→{completed,cancelled,no_show}; completed/cancelled terminal; no_show→completed (reversible). Disallowed transition → 422 ValidationError. Repo write-methods are status-guarded in the WHERE clause (defense-in-depth). Property-tested in appointment.fsm.property.test.ts.

items.title: Cancellation reason required min:5/max:500 (BR-SCH-003 / REASON_REQUIRED)
items.notes: One canonical cancel policy (FIX-002/GAP-6): DELETE reads ?reason query param, PATCH reads body.cancellationReason; trimmed length <5 or >500 → 422 BusinessLogicError REASON_REQUIRED on either path.

items.title: Cancel via PATCH restricted to dentist_owner/staff_full (close role bypass)
items.notes: PATCH {status:'cancelled'} re-asserts assertBranchRole(['dentist_owner','staff_full']) — the broad write gate admits staff_scheduling/associate for reschedule but they must NOT cancel through PATCH. Enforces the SAME reason rule (5-500 → 422 REASON_REQUIRED), SAME AL-008 audit + DE-011 event as the DELETE cancel path.

items.title: Check-in blocked when patient has active visit (V-SCH-002 / CHECKIN_ACTIVE_VISIT 409)
items.notes: EC7 race: two concurrent check-ins for the same patient both pass the pre-tx guard (unique index only covers status='active', not the fresh 'draft'). Inside the tx: pg_advisory_xact_lock(2001, hashtext(patientId)) serializes per patient, then re-asserts findInProgressVisitByPatient under the lock; loser re-reads the winner's committed draft and aborts 409. Tested in checkInAppointment.concurrency.test.ts.

items.title: Hygienist may check in ONLY hygiene-typed appointments (E3)
items.notes: derivedVisitType = serviceType==='hygiene' ? 'hygiene' : 'general'. Hygiene appt → check-in roles include 'hygienist' (yields hygiene visit); general appt → owner/associate/staff_full only (hygienist NOT appended). assertBranchRole on the derived set.

items.title: Confirm-by-token single-use idempotency (replay → 404)
items.notes: Single-atomic UPDATE: only matches a still-scheduled appointment with the exact token, clears the token on success. Unknown/used token or non-scheduled status → null → 404. confirmationToken column has a partial-unique index (WHERE NOT NULL); ensureConfirmationToken mints idempotently.

items.title: Create appointment authz: scheduling-capable role in target branch (EM-SCH-001)
items.notes: RLS WITH CHECK only validates the appointment's own branch_id; caller-supplied body.patientId is resolved via getPatientForDentalPatient → assertPatientBranchAccess on patient.preferredBranchId before insert/notify. Unknown patient → 404; foreign-branch patient → 403. Prevents scheduling a foreign-branch patient into this branch.

items.title: endAt strictly after startAt + visitType enum on create (V-SCH-007/008)
items.notes: V-SCH-008: endAt.getTime() must be > startAt.getTime() else 422 ValidationError. V-SCH-007: isVisitType guards visitType to {checkup,treatment,emergency,recall,hygiene} else 422. durationMinutes derived from the start/end range.

items.title: Double-booking soft-warn at create (FR3.7 / DOUBLE_BOOKING)
items.notes: findOverlapping for the provider/branch/window; overlap → 201 with warnings:['DOUBLE_BOOKING'] (non-blocking) + a dental-scheduling.double-booking WARN log (no PII). Same soft posture used in promoteWaitlistEntry. Contrast: reschedule + online booking HARD-block.

items.title: Online booking policy gates (enabled/visit-type/provider/auth/lead/horizon/hours)
items.notes: Branch inactive/missing → 404. ONLINE_BOOKING_DISABLED if !config.enabled. VISIT_TYPE_NOT_BOOKABLE if not isOnlineBookable. PATIENT_AUTH_REQUIRED when config.requirePatientAuth (prospect bookings hard-gated today). provider must be a branch online-bookable provider (else 422). SLOT_TOO_SOON (lead time), SLOT_BEYOND_HORIZON (horizon days), OUTSIDE_WORKING_HOURS. firstName required.

items.title: Online booking hard-block double-booking (SLOT_TAKEN/SLOT_HELD + EXCLUDE backstop)
items.notes: Unlike staff (soft-warn), online booking HARD-blocks: inside the commit tx final overlap re-check vs appointments → 409 SLOT_TAKEN and vs active holds (ignoring caller's own by sessionToken) → 409 SLOT_HELD. Postgres EXCLUDE-USING-gist constraint (dental_appointment_no_overlap, code 23P01 on err or err.cause) is the storage backstop → mapped to friendly 409 SLOT_TAKEN. Caller's own hold released (deleteByToken) on success.

items.title: Only an active waitlist entry can be promoted (WAITLIST_ENTRY_NOT_ACTIVE)
items.notes: WAITLIST_ENTRY_FSM: active→{scheduled,cancelled}; scheduled/cancelled terminal. Entry status !== 'active' → 422 WAITLIST_ENTRY_NOT_ACTIVE. repo.promote is status-guarded; a lost race (entry no longer active) → 422 WAITLIST_ENTRY_NOT_ACTIVE inside the tx (single-promotion guarantee).

items.title: Public booking endpoints rate-limited (abuse guard)
items.notes: Unauthenticated public routes guarded by per-IP limiters (bookingWriteLimiter for write/confirm, availabilityLimiter for read) keyed by clientIp(headers) + operation/branch. Exceeded → 429 RateLimitError. Defined in public-booking-ratelimit.ts.

items.title: Queue item FSM enforcement (INVALID_FSM_TRANSITION)
items.notes: QUEUE_ITEM_FSM table in queue-item.schema.ts. Any status not in QUEUE_ITEM_FSM[current] → 422 BusinessLogicError INVALID_FSM_TRANSITION. Terminal states (completed, cancelled) accept no further transitions.

items.title: Reminder consent gate — fails closed per channel (resolveConsentedChannels)
items.notes: A channel is eligible iff in branch policy AND patient consented. Outbound (sms/email/push) FAIL CLOSED unless explicit true; push has no flag → always closed. in-app always allowed. Reminders transactional (marketing NOT required) but a global opt-out (all known outbound false) suppresses outbound (in-app remains). preferredChannel honored as primary (drops other outbound when eligible). Suppressions audited with no-PII reason. Pure fn, unit-tested.

items.title: Reminder enqueue idempotency (no duplicates on re-run)
items.notes: enqueueScheduledIfAbsent is keyed on (relatedEntity, type, channel, scheduledAt) so re-runs write zero duplicates. Lead times in the past are skipped. Mutations to an appointment expire its now-stale queued reminders (expireQueuedByEntity over REMINDER_NOTIFICATION_TYPES) on confirm/cancel/no_show/check-in/reschedule; the armer re-arms for the new time.

items.title: Reschedule hard-block on overlap (V-SCH-001 / RESCHEDULE_CONFLICT 409)
items.notes: When the time window moves, findOverlapping (excluding self) inside the tenant tx; any overlap → 409 ConflictError RESCHEDULE_CONFLICT (NOT generic CONFLICT, NOT a soft warning). Overlap re-check + field update share one withTenantTx.

items.title: RLS branch isolation on scheduling writes/reads (P1b activation)
items.notes: Every dental_appointment / dental_queue_item / dental_waitlist_entry / dental_appointment_hold read+write is routed through withTenantTx({branchIds}) so the app_rls policy enforces the branch scope as a second wall (WITH CHECK validates inserts). Entity-resolution fetches + authz + FSM/working-hours guards intentionally stay on db to preserve exact 403/404/409/422. Role-probe RED tests in dental-scheduling.rls-activation-appointments.test.ts + dental-scheduling.rls-activation-queue-waitlist.test.ts.

items.title: Walk-in bypasses working-hours validation (BR-SCH-002)
items.notes: When body.walkIn=true the configured working-hours check (FR3.10) is skipped; non-walk-in must pass isWithinWorkingHours.

items.title: Appointment must be within branch working hours (BR-SCH-004 / OUTSIDE_WORKING_HOURS)
items.notes: Non-walk-in create + reschedule (time window moved) re-validate against parseWorkingHours/isWithinWorkingHours (timezone-aware via Intl). Out-of-hours → 422 BusinessLogicError OUTSIDE_WORKING_HOURS. getBranchSchedulingConfig facade (dental-org).

items.title: BR-001: No two active visits per patient
items.notes: App-level findActiveByPatient guard returns 409 ACTIVE_VISIT_EXISTS (not a raw 500 from the partial unique index) on create AND on draft->active; enforced globally per patient across branches. Discard is the documented escape hatch.

items.title: BR-002: Visit transitions are strictly linear
items.notes: VISIT_TRANSITIONS map (draft->active->completed->locked; active->discarded server-only) enforced; illegal transition -> 422 VISIT_TRANSITION_INVALID. No reversal.

items.title: BR-003: Visit immutable after completed/locked
items.notes: Cross-cutting lock gate: every chart/treatment/finding/notes/template/carry-over write path rejects completed/locked visits with 422 VISIT_IMMUTABLE (VISIT_LOCKED on the visit PATCH). localId replays are allowed to resolve idempotently past the gate.

items.title: BR-005: Auto-discard empty visit (flag-gated)
items.notes: When DENTAL_VISIT_AUTO_DISCARD=true, an active->completed with no treatments, no meaningful SOAP content, and no attachments redirects to discarded instead. Default OFF (V-VIS-004, ADR-010).

items.title: BR-006: Treatment transitions forward-only
items.notes: TREATMENT_TRANSITIONS: diagnosed->planned->performed->verified; dismissed/declined terminal & reachable from non-terminal; illegal -> 422. Sync-apply path uses monotonic mergeTreatmentStatus (rank) so a stale offline status can't regress a newer one.

items.title: BR-007: Performed/verified treatment field-immutable
items.notes: On performed/verified, editing cdtCode/toothNumber/surfaces/description/conditionCode -> 422 TREATMENT_IMMUTABLE; status transitions and sequencing metadata (phase/priority) still allowed. priceCents locked at creation (updates ignored).

items.title: BR-008: Carry-over creates new rows (carriedOver flag)
items.notes: Carry-over inserts NEW dental_treatment rows on the next visit with carriedOver=true and sourceVisitId=<prior visit>; display-only provenance, the source rows are unchanged.

items.title: BR-014: Signed consent required before performing treatment / completing visit
items.notes: hasSignedConsentForVisit (dental-clinical facade) gates marking a treatment performed (TREATMENT_CONSENT_REQUIRED) and completing a non-empty visit (VISIT_CONSENT_REQUIRED).

items.title: EF-PAT-001: Cannot accept plan for an archived patient
items.notes: Accepting a plan on an archived patient -> 422 PATIENT_ARCHIVED; consentFormId (if supplied) validated to belong to the patient before the append-only version is created (no orphan version).

items.title: BR-VIS-009: apply-template is clinical-role gated and branch-scoped
items.notes: Applying a template injects billable treatments, so it requires owner/associate (not mere assertBranchAccess); the template must be active AND belong to the visit's branch — a foreign-branch template is treated as 404 (no cross-clinic CDT/pricing leak, audit 2026-06-08).

items.title: Stale offline chart write persisted as durable sync conflict (not dropped)
items.notes: Clock-aware LWW baseline merge; a tooth that loses the clock is recorded on the chart (syncStatus='conflict' + conflictPayload.rejectedTeeth) rather than silently dropped (SL-12/F-G04), so it is surfaceable + resolvable.

items.title: Odontogram is a living document — every chart write merges into patient baseline
items.notes: CHART-BR-002: both full-upsert and per-tooth PATCH merge the full post-edit teeth array into the cumulative patient baseline; existing/existing_other entries are immutable in the merge; GET inherits baseline when the visit has no chart (carry-over).

items.title: Treatment price defaults from branch fee schedule when omitted
items.notes: When priceCents omitted, resolveFeeCents picks branch override > catalog defaultFeePhp > 0 (AC-ORG-002); an explicit price always wins. Money invariant on a clinical write.

items.title: Finding conditionCode='other' requires a note
items.notes: conditionCode 'other' (free-text) requires a non-empty note on create and when transitioning to 'other' on edit (merged code/note), else 422 ValidationError.

items.title: Offline-replay idempotency via client localId (GAP-001/SL-01)
items.notes: A retried create carrying a previously-seen localId returns the EXISTING row (visit/chart/treatment/finding) instead of inserting a duplicate; resolves before the active-visit guard and before the immutability gate; a partial unique index (branchId,localId) guards the concurrent-retry race for visits.

items.title: Signing a note locks it; corrections via addendum only
items.notes: Signed note is immutable: re-upsert -> NOTE_SIGNED, re-sign -> NOTE_ALREADY_SIGNED; v1 snapshot frozen; addendum requires note already signed (NOTE_NOT_SIGNED) + reason; every sign/amend is audited.

items.title: EC2: Cannot add treatment to extracted tooth
items.notes: If the visit chart marks the tooth state 'extracted', creating a pending treatment on it -> 422 TOOTH_EXTRACTED.

items.title: Decline treatment requires refusalReason (informed refusal)
items.notes: status='declined' requires a non-empty refusalReason (REFUSAL_REASON_REQUIRED); legally-significant terminal transition, audited with refusalReason; recompute plan denominator.

items.title: Dismiss treatment captures reason + audit + plan recompute
items.notes: status='dismissed' records dismissReason (default 'Dismissed'), writes treatment.dismissed audit, and recomputes the parent plan completion denominator (TR-P1-08).

items.title: BR-VIS-010: Treatment-plan endpoints authorize against patient's branch (V-VIS-011)
items.notes: branchId stays a required contract field but is NOT the auth boundary; auth resolves the patient and gates on its preferred branch (assertPatientBranchAccess). Gating on the query branchId previously let a foreign-branch caller read/snapshot another branch's patient plan (cross-tenant PHI leak, audit 2026-06-08).

items.title: Treatment plan versions are append-only immutable snapshots
items.notes: Each accept writes a new monotonically-versioned TreatmentPlanVersion (createSnapshotVersion, collision-retry, un-tx'd); prior versions never mutated; version row reads require patientId match (cross-patient version-access guard).

items.title: Cross-tenant patient-link guard on visit create (P1-6)
items.notes: body.patientId is caller-supplied and RLS WITH CHECK only validates the new visit's own branch_id; handler resolves the patient and requires the caller to belong to ITS branch (assertPatientBranchAccess) so a foreign-branch patient can't be linked into this branch.

items.title: All email endpoints require admin role
items.notes: authMiddleware({roles:['admin']}) on every route AND a defense-in-depth parseUserRoles(user).includes('admin') check inside each handler -> ForbiddenError. No-user/no-roles denied gracefully (403, no crash).

items.title: Cancel guard: cannot cancel sent or already-cancelled
items.notes: cancelEmail: reason required non-empty <=500 chars (ValidationError); status='sent' -> 422 EMAIL_ALREADY_SENT; status='cancelled' -> 422 EMAIL_ALREADY_CANCELLED. 404 if not found.

items.title: Cleanup deletes only old sent/cancelled emails
items.notes: cleanupOldEmails(daysOld=30) deletes only rows with status in [sent,cancelled] AND createdAt<=cutoff; never removes pending/processing/failed. Returns deleted count.

items.title: Template create requires name, subject, bodyHtml
items.notes: createEmailTemplate throws ValidationError if name/subject/bodyHtml missing (zValidator also rejects). variables optional, defaults to []; status defaults 'draft'.

items.title: Template Handlebars syntax must compile
items.notes: EmailTemplateRepository.validateTemplateSyntax compiles subject/bodyHtml/bodyText via Handlebars; compile failure -> ValidationError. On update only re-validated when content fields change.

items.title: Pending selection: priority order, schedule + cancel awareness
items.notes: getPendingEmails: excludes cancelledAt rows; selects pending (scheduledAt null or <=now) OR failed-ready-for-retry; orders by priority asc then createdAt asc; limit 50. Drives processing fairness/scheduling.

items.title: Queued email must reference a template or templateTags
items.notes: EmailQueueRepository.queueEmail throws ValidationError unless template OR templateTags supplied; recipient length capped at 255 (varchar(255)); EmailService.queueEmail (cross-module path) further requires non-empty templateTags. Defaults priority=5, status='pending', attempts=0.

items.title: Send-time template resolution by tag (active only)
items.notes: EmailServiceImpl.resolveTemplateByTags loads status='active' templates and matches the first whose tags array intersects the queued templateTags; no active match -> processEmail throws and the row is marked failed. Sender/replyTo overrides come from the resolved template.

items.title: Failure retry uses exponential backoff capped at 3 attempts
items.notes: markAsFailed increments attempts and sets nextRetryAt via calculateNextRetryTime (5m/30m/2h backoff); attempts>=3 -> nextRetryAt null (no more retries). getPendingEmails re-picks failed rows only while nextRetryAt<=now AND attempts<=3.

items.title: Retry guard: only failed + under max attempts
items.notes: retryEmail: status must be 'failed' (else 422 INVALID_STATUS_FOR_RETRY); attempts<3 (else 422 MAX_RETRIES_EXCEEDED); resets to pending. 404 if not found.

items.title: Template update bumps version and invalidates cache
items.notes: updateTemplate increments version=(existing.version||1)+1 and calls invalidateCache(id) so the 5-minute in-memory compiled-template cache is dropped on edit.

items.title: Test email requires an active template + valid recipient
items.notes: testEmailTemplate: recipientEmail required + regex-valid (ValidationError otherwise); getActiveTemplate must return a non-null active template else 404 'not found or not active'.

items.title: Template variable definitions must be well-formed
items.notes: validateVariableDefinitions: no duplicate variable ids; each needs id+type+label; string options array cannot be empty; number min cannot exceed max. Violation -> ValidationError.

items.title: Render-time variable validation against definitions
items.notes: validateVariables enforces required vars present and per-type rules (string min/max/pattern/options; number min/max; boolean; date/datetime; email regex; url URL-parse; array). renderTemplate aggregates errors -> ValidationError; reused by processEmail send path.

items.title: BR: audit tenant slot is non-PHI sentinel + PHI-safe metadata
items.notes: V-EMR-005: audit row tenantId uses EMR_AUDIT_TENANT_SENTINEL when null, NEVER the patient UUID (no PHI id in tenant slot). All six ops persist a dental_audit_log row via logAuditEvent with dotted-lowercase emr.<resource>.<verb> action names (V-EMR-006): create/finalize/update/read/list (V-EMR-002/003/004). Update audit records field NAMES only, never PHI values (V-EMR-003). Inter-module write into audit subsystem.

items.title: BR: authoring/finalizing gated to owning provider
items.notes: V-EMR-AUTH: only a provider may create, and only as themselves (body.provider must resolve to caller's own provider profile else 403; caller without provider profile → PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND 422). Update/finalize are provider:owner — non-owning provider → 403. Route-level provider:owner role is non-gating by design (authMiddleware delegates ownership to handler, the real boundary).

items.title: BR: clinical text length + finalized-row integrity DB constraints
items.notes: DB check constraints (emr.schema.ts): chiefComplaint 1..500, assessment 1..2000, plan 1..2000 (all when non-null). finalizedAtConstraint enforces status='finalized' rows MUST have finalizedAt AND finalizedBy not null (signed-record integrity). No DB-level FK to patient/provider/finalizedBy (loose cross-module coupling per MODULE_SPEC §20).

items.title: BR: context idempotency key is unique
items.notes: V-EMR-CTX: when supplied, context (e.g. appointment:123) is unique across consultation notes — duplicate → CONSULTATION_EXISTS / DB unique-constraint conflict (consultation_notes_context_unique). context optional; absent → no uniqueness. Checked in handler (findByContext) + createDirect + DB unique index.

items.title: BR: finalize is terminal (draft→finalized only, no amend)
items.notes: V-EMR-001 FSM: draft→finalized is the ONLY valid transition; finalized AND amended are terminal (no outgoing). Finalizing non-draft → 422 CONSULTATION_NOT_DRAFT; updating finalized → 422 CONSULTATION_NOT_DRAFT (immutable signed record). 'amended' enum value reserved/unreachable (no producer); struck draft→finalized→amended→finalized machine. Enforced in emr.repo.ts validateStatusTransition + handler guards. Covered by fast-check property test.

items.title: BR: isolation by provider/patient ownership (no branch/tenant)
items.notes: V-EMR-OWN headline isolation invariant: no branch/tenant boundary; provider reads/updates/finalizes ONLY own notes, patient reads ONLY own, admin all. getConsultation = admin|provider:owner|patient:owner else 403. List endpoints force scope from session; patient passing another patient's ?patient= → 403 (scope cannot be widened by client param).

items.title: Distance/Area tools blocked until calibrated
items.notes: measurement-toolbar.tsx CALIBRATION_REQUIRED_TOOLS={distance,area}: buttons disabled + amber banner when !isCalibrated; never silently falls back to px. "Calibrate now" switches toolMode to calibration. Patient-safety guard (no fake mm).

items.title: Distance/Area tools blocked until calibrated
items.notes: measurement-toolbar.tsx CALIBRATION_REQUIRED_TOOLS={distance,area}: buttons disabled + amber banner when !isCalibrated; never silently falls back to px. "Calibrate now" switches toolMode to calibration. Patient-safety guard (no fake mm).

items.title: Only dentists may confirm landmarks + generate the report

userEntryPoint.control: finalize controls hidden / data-testid=ceph-draft-only-notice for non-dentist roles

items.notes: canFinalize = role in {dentist_owner, dentist_associate} (org-context store); assistants see "Drafting only" notice. Mirrors backend G4-B sign-off split (403 otherwise). Inter-module FE/BE authz boundary.

items.title: Only dentists may confirm landmarks + generate the report

userEntryPoint.control: finalize controls hidden / data-testid=ceph-draft-only-notice for non-dentist roles

items.notes: canFinalize = role in {dentist_owner, dentist_associate} (org-context store); assistants see "Drafting only" notice. Mirrors backend G4-B sign-off split (403 otherwise). Inter-module FE/BE authz boundary.

items.title: Advance a finding's status (draft → confirmed → resolved)
items.notes: FindingsSidebar handleCycleStatus → nextStatus forward-only (resolved terminal, no wrap → avoids 422) → updateFinding (imagingFindingsMgmtUpdateFinding). V-IMG-007 SM-01 FSM. Clinical status transition → full layer set.

items.title: Advance a finding's status (draft → confirmed → resolved)
items.notes: FindingsSidebar handleCycleStatus → nextStatus forward-only (resolved terminal, no wrap → avoids 422) → updateFinding (imagingFindingsMgmtUpdateFinding). V-IMG-007 SM-01 FSM. Clinical status transition → full layer set.

items.title: Choose superimposition registration reference
items.notes: SuperimpositionPanel: only cranial_base (S–N) is v1-enabled; maxillary/mandibular are disabled ("v2"). Changing reference re-runs the preview mutation. Clinical-honesty constraint (two-point registration, not ABO-grade).

items.title: Choose superimposition registration reference
items.notes: SuperimpositionPanel: only cranial_base (S–N) is v1-enabled; maxillary/mandibular are disabled ("v2"). Changing reference re-runs the preview mutation. Clinical-honesty constraint (two-point registration, not ABO-grade).

items.title: Validate upload file type/size before submit
items.notes: image-upload.tsx validateFile: ALLOWED_TYPES = JPEG/PNG/TIFF/BMP/DICOM (+.dcm extension signal), MAX 100MB. Rejects with inline error ("Unsupported format…" / "File too large…"); Upload button disabled while error/uploading. Pure client guard, no endpoint.

items.title: Validate upload file type/size before submit
items.notes: image-upload.tsx validateFile: ALLOWED_TYPES = JPEG/PNG/TIFF/BMP/DICOM (+.dcm extension signal), MAX 100MB. Rejects with inline error ("Unsupported format…" / "File too large…"); Upload button disabled while error/uploading. Pure client guard, no endpoint.

items.title: Inbox 'Showing N of M unread' truncation indicator
items.notes: When unreadCount > rendered rows (>25 unread), shows honest 'Showing X of Y unread' footer instead of silently capping the page. Hidden while loading/error/no-overflow.

items.title: Inbox 'Showing N of M unread' truncation indicator
items.notes: When unreadCount > rendered rows (>25 unread), shows honest 'Showing X of Y unread' footer instead of silently capping the page. Hidden while loading/error/no-overflow.

items.title: Push opt-in nudge gating / visibility
items.notes: shouldShowPushOptIn = enabled && !granted && !dismissed. Renders NOTHING when OneSignal unconfigured/uninitialized, permission already granted, or per-device dismissed flag (localStorage dentalemon.pushOptInDismissed) is set. Mounted in _workspace shell per Q2 (prompt at first relevant clinical action, not at login). No backend call on its own — pure client gating over browser/OneSignal permission state.

items.title: Push opt-in nudge gating / visibility

Mounted in _workspace shell per Q2 (prompt at first relevant clinical action, not at login). No backend call on its own — pure client gating over browser/OneSignal permission state.

items.title: Unread notification badge count
items.notes: Badge = pagination.totalCount of the unread query (authoritative); visual text capped to 9+ and aria-hidden, exact count exposed via the bell aria-label only. Auto-refreshes every 60s (refetchInterval, V1 polling, no websockets). Badge hidden when unreadCount===0.

items.title: Unread notification badge count
items.notes: Badge = pagination.totalCount of the unread query (authoritative); visual text capped to 9+ and aria-hidden, exact count exposed via the bell aria-label only. Auto-refreshes every 60s (refetchInterval, V1 polling, no websockets). Badge hidden when unreadCount===0.

items.title: Notification type → email template tag mapping (with Phase-2 dormant tags)
items.notes: notification-classification.ts mapNotificationTypeToEmailTemplate (FIX-003). security→auth.password-reset, system→auth.welcome are REGISTERED; appointment.reminder/confirmation-request, recall.due/reminder map to tags that are intentionally NOT yet backed by a registered .hbs template (PHASE2_UNREGISTERED_EMAIL_TEMPLATE_TAGS). Unmapped types → null (no email). Registry-invariant test guards against a NEW mapping pointing at a phantom template.

items.title: Idempotent scheduled-notification enqueue (duplicate-send guard)
items.notes: repo.enqueueScheduledIfAbsent (P1-24). Keys on (type, channel, relatedEntity, scheduledAt) — null-safe via isNull. If any row with that exact key exists (ANY status), returns {created:false, existing} and writes nothing; else creates. The duplicate-send guard the reminder/recall jobs (reminderArmer, recallDispatch) rely on so re-runs never double-send. Inter-module + patient-safety adjacent.

items.title: Expire queued reminders when their entity moves on
items.notes: repo.expireQueuedByEntity (P1-24). Called inside cancel/reschedule/check-in/confirm appointment handlers (dental-scheduling) so a reminder/confirmation-request can never fire after the appointment moved on. Only touches status='queued' rows for the given relatedEntity+types; delivered/read left intact for history. Returns rows-expired count.

items.title: Notification final-state derivation on create (immediate in-app vs queued)
items.notes: createNotificationForModule: an immediate (no scheduledAt or scheduledAt<=now) in-app notification is written directly as status='sent' with sentAt=now in a single op; scheduled or non-in-app notifications are written as status='queued' sentAt=null. Determines whether the background processor will pick the row up.

items.title: List defaults to in-app channel when no channel filter given
items.notes: buildWhereConditions: when no channel filter is supplied the query auto-restricts to channel='in-app' (so the inbox never shows email/push/sms rows). status='unread' is a virtual value mapping to status IN (sent,delivered).

items.title: Medical/safety-critical notification-type classifier
items.notes: notification-classification.ts isMedicalNotificationType — pure, set-injectable. MEDICAL_NOTIFICATION_TYPES is intentionally EMPTY in V1 (real classifier, not the former hardcoded false). Drives consent-validation warning and push priority=10. Ready for the first registered medical type.

items.title: Consent validation for medical/safety-critical notifications
items.notes: createNotificationForModule: if isMedicalNotification(type) && !consentValidated, logs a warning (fail-open — does not block). consentValidated persisted to row (default false). V1 defines NO medical types (MEDICAL_NOTIFICATION_TYPES is empty) so this is currently inert but live.

items.title: Elevated push priority for medical/safety-critical types
items.notes: deliverNotification push case: isMedicalNotification(type) sets OneSignal priority=10 (FIX-004 fail-open). Currently inert (no medical types defined) but the classifier is live, not a hardcoded constant.

items.title: Recipient ownership scoping (anti-IDOR) on all reads/mutations
items.notes: Every list/get/mark operation is scoped to user.id as recipient: findManyByRecipient, findOneByIdAndRecipient, markAsRead (verify ownership → 404), markAllAsRead (recipient WHERE). A user can never read or mutate another recipient's notifications; not-found and no-access are intentionally indistinguishable (security).

items.title: SMS channel is enum-now / provider-later no-op (marked failed)
items.notes: deliverNotification 'sms' case (P1-24): no SMS provider wired (Twilio/Vonage deferred to P4); logs a warning and sets status='failed' (mirrors OneSignal-not-configured). Dunning/recall consent+cadence logic is unaffected — SMS is intentionally a logged no-op.

items.title: Notification status FSM (queued→sent→delivered→read / failed / expired)
items.notes: Status enum queued/sent/delivered/read/failed/expired. Transitions: create→sent (immediate in-app) or queued; processScheduled→sent→delivered (or failed); markAsRead/markAllAsRead→read (only from sent/delivered); expireQueuedByEntity queued→expired; delivery error→failed. markAllAsRead deliberately excludes queued/scheduled rows (only sent/delivered eligible).

items.title: Existing active clinic (409) drops straight to dashboard
items.notes: On SdkError status 409 (already have a fully-provisioned clinic), wizard removes STORAGE_KEY and calls onComplete() -> /dashboard instead of erroring. Prevents a localStorage resume from trapping the user in a 409 loop. Tenant-state edge -> all 4 layers.

items.notes: SdkError 403 with code EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED surfaces 'Please verify your email address before creating a clinic.' Security/access-gating rule -> all 4 layers.

PIN and patient fields are intentionally NOT persisted (security/privacy). A 409 on finish clears STORAGE_KEY so a stale resume can't trap the user.

items.title: Cannot archive a patient with an active payment plan (EC1)
items.notes: patient.repo.ts archivePatient L140-145: if patient.hasActivePaymentPlan return failure → handler throws PATIENT_ARCHIVE_BLOCKED. hasActivePaymentPlan is written by dental-billing via setPatientActivePaymentPlanFlag facade (cross-module money invariant). Already-archived => failure too.

items.title: Archive/restore concurrency guard (atomic WHERE on status)
items.notes: patient.repo.ts archivePatient L152-160 / restorePatient L179-187: UPDATE ... WHERE id AND status='active' (or 'archived'). Concurrent calls that both read the same status both attempt the update but only one row returns; the loser gets 'Patient status changed concurrently'. TOCTOU protection.

items.title: Create blocked without a person identity (MISSING_PERSON_PROFILE)
items.notes: createPatient.ts L27-29: if !user.id throw BusinessLogicError MISSING_PERSON_PROFILE. Guards that a person profile exists before a patient profile is created.

items.title: Deactivate enforces patient-branch membership (IDOR fix)
items.notes: deactivatePatient.ts L29-40 (P1-3). Handler previously had ZERO authz; route admits bare 'user' → any user could archive any patient. Now requires assertPatientBranchAccess; branchless patient => 403.

items.title: Duplicate-patient detection & clustering (FR2.5 / P2-16)
items.notes: patient.repo.ts findPotentialDuplicates (non-blocking name hint, max 5) and findDuplicateCandidates (clusters by strong key name+DOB, name+shared-contact, or name-only). Surfaced via patient-dental-patient.facade findDuplicatePatientGroups (PII-minimal shape). Route /patients/duplicates lives in dental-patient module but is powered by this repo. Surfaces merge candidates only; merge itself is BR-020 deferred.

items.title: Get patient authorization — owner self or staff role only (else 403)
items.notes: getPatient.ts L70-81: isOwner = patient.person===user.id; staff = admin/clinician/registrar/support; non-owner non-staff => ForbiddenError. Patient portal can only ever read OWN record.

items.title: Patient search matches full "first last" display name
items.notes: patient-person.facade.ts listConditions L41-53: ilike against firstName, lastName, AND concat(firstName||' '||lastName) so a natural full-name query ("Maria Santos") matches (FR2.2 regression).

items.title: List is strictly per-branch — branchless patients never leak across branches
items.notes: patient-person.facade.ts L55-64: branchId/branchIds filter does NOT OR-in isNull(preferredBranchId). Prior code leaked every branchless patient into every branch's list (cross-tenant PHI leak); now branchless patients surface in no branch list.

items.title: Merge/unmerge are admin-only (non-admin → 403)
items.notes: mergePatients.ts L19-21 / unmergePatients.ts L19-21: UnauthorizedError if no user; ForbiddenError if user.role !== 'admin'. RBAC checked before the 501 short-circuit.

items.title: One patient profile per person (PATIENT_EXISTS / unique index)
items.notes: createPatient.ts L90-93 findByPersonId pre-check throws BusinessLogicError PATIENT_EXISTS; enforced structurally by patients_person_id_unique uniqueIndex in patient.schema.ts L55.

items.title: Self-registration grants patient role; staff creation does not
items.notes: createPatient.ts L104-114: when caller is NOT admin/clinician/registrar/support, addUserRole(db,user,'patient') is called; staff-created patients skip the role grant. Role takes effect on next session.

items.title: Update enforces patient-branch membership (IDOR fix)
items.notes: updatePatient.ts L48-53 assertPatientBranchAccess (P1-2). Route admits bare 'user'; previously isOwner was only logged and the mutation ran unconditionally → any user could write any patient row. Branchless/foreign-branch patient => 403.

items.title: Persist per-channel communication consent (post-registration)
items.notes: persistCommunicationConsentWithRetry (communication-consent.ts). Registration has already committed when this runs → failure is non-fatal to the record but MUST be visible (error toast + Retry action; success toast only on a successful retry). Phase-2 reminders act on this consent, so a silent failure is a trust defect. Never throws.

Country defaults via detectCountry().

items.notes: personalInfoSchema (zodResolver): firstName/lastName required, max 50; middleName optional max 50 (or empty string); dateOfBirth required Date refined to age 0..150; gender optional enum [male, female, non-binary, other, prefer-not-to-say] (or empty); Calendar restricts selectable dates to <= today and >= 1900-01-01. DOB serialized to YYYY-MM-DD (plainDate) via toPlainDateString before send; gender normalized via normalizeGender.

items.title: BR: create/update emit a privacy audit event; audit failure never blocks the write
items.notes: audit.logEvent (eventType data-modification, category privacy) records actor, resource, updatedFields/hasEmail/hasPhone, ip/userAgent. Wrapped in try/catch so an audit-sink failure is logged but does not fail the mutation. PII not logged in plaintext (only hasEmail/hasPhone booleans).

items.title: BR: one person profile per user — duplicate create → 409 Conflict
items.notes: Pre-insert findOneById(user.id); if a Person already exists → ConflictError 'User already has a person profile' (409). Enforces uniqueness of the 1:1 relationship.

items.title: BR: createPerson binds person.id to the authenticated user (self-provision only)
items.notes: Handler always sets person.id = user.id (1:1 Person↔User invariant). No path/body accepts an arbitrary id — a caller cannot create a profile for someone else.

items.title: BR: dateOfBirth must be valid, not future-dated, not before 1900
items.notes: Handler-level validateDateOfBirth/validatedDateOfBirth: invalid date, future date, or date < 1900-01-01 → ValidationError (400/422). Applied on create and on update (when DOB provided and not null). Patient-safety-adjacent (age-dependent clinical logic).

items.title: BR: anonymizePersonPii is idempotent (already-erased = no-op) and reports state
items.notes: A second call on an already-erased person (firstName === ERASED_MARKER) returns {anonymized:false, alreadyErased:true}; missing person → {anonymized:false, alreadyErased:false}. isPersonErased exposes the erased flag via the [ERASED] sentinel.

items.title: BR: cross-module access to Person goes only through typed facades (Phase 10 boundary lint)
items.notes: Consumer modules import only the *.facade.ts bridges (billing/notifs/provisioning/dental-patient/erasure), never person.repo or person.schema directly. Enforced by the Phase 10 boundary lint; keeps Person as the single PII safeguard.

items.title: BR: firstName is required (validator-level) → 400
items.notes: CreatePersonBody zValidator rejects a missing firstName with 400 before the handler runs. firstName column is NOT NULL (varchar 50).

items.title: BR: gender constrained to enum [male,female,non-binary,other,prefer-not-to-say]
items.notes: pgEnum 'gender' + validator constrain allowed values; PATCH may set it to null to clear.

items.title: BR: getPerson — internal-expand requests skip per-record authz
items.notes: When ctx.isInternalExpand is set (composed read from a parent resource already authorized, e.g. EMR note expand), the owner check is bypassed. Risk surface: bypass must only be reachable from authorized parent flows.

items.title: BR: getPerson — 'me' resolves to caller's own id; rejected for internal-expand
items.notes: personId === 'me' is rewritten to user.id; if there is no authenticated user (internal expand) → ValidationError '"me" not supported for internal expand requests'.

items.title: BR: getPerson — missing record returns 404 even for a non-owner (404 before 403)
items.notes: findOneById runs before the authorization check, so a non-existent person yields NotFoundError 404 (not 403). Avoids leaking existence via the authz path.

items.title: BR: getPerson — non-owner is denied (PII safeguard) → 403
items.notes: Outside internal-expand, only the owner (user.id === personId) may read; otherwise ForbiddenError 403. Person is the central PII safeguard. NOTE: route also grants admin/support roles at middleware level.

items.title: BR: listPersons — q search, sort whitelist, pagination bounds
items.notes: q does ilike across firstName+lastName; sort restricted to [firstName,lastName,createdAt,updatedAt] (default createdAt:desc) — non-whitelisted sort fields ignored; pagination via parsePagination.

items.title: BR: updatePerson — contactInfo is a partial merge, not a full JSONB replace
items.notes: Provided contactInfo sub-fields override; omitted ones keep stored values ({...existing, ...body.contactInfo}). Fix for prior silent data loss (a phone-only edit no longer wipes the stored email). Mirrors the dental-patient facade. ISSUE-030.

items.title: BR: updatePerson — target must exist → 404
items.notes: findOneById before update; missing → NotFoundError 404 with suggestions.

items.title: BR: updatePerson — only the owner may update their profile → 403
items.notes: user.id !== personId → ForbiddenError 'You can only update your own profile' (403). Route middleware also restricts to user:owner.

items.title: BR: updatePerson — undefined = no change, null = explicit clear (partial PATCH)
items.notes: updateData is built field-by-field only for keys present in the body; omitted fields are untouched, explicit null clears. Prevents accidental wipes on partial edits.

items.title: Preview import — validate source fields + JSON content
items.notes: Client-side business rules before advancing to preview: Source Facility required; Source Software/System required and <=200 chars (provenance audit trail); PMD content required and must be valid JSON. On valid input extracts conditions/medications/allergies Safety Floor preview. No network call until Confirm.

items.title: Patient portal auth guard (authenticated, no PIN/membership)
items.notes: _portal.tsx beforeLoad calls requireAuth only — deliberately does NOT require staff PIN session or org membership (those gate _dashboard). Ownership of data is enforced server-side by every /me endpoint via assertSelfPatient. Unauthenticated users are redirected to sign-in by requireAuth.

items.title: Patient portal auth guard (authenticated, no PIN/membership)
items.notes: _portal.tsx beforeLoad calls requireAuth only — deliberately does NOT require staff PIN session or org membership (those gate _dashboard). Ownership of data is enforced server-side by every /me endpoint via assertSelfPatient. Unauthenticated users are redirected to sign-in by requireAuth.

items.title: BR — createPractitioner requires an existing parent Provider (FK guard)
items.notes: createPractitioner validates the supplied providerId resolves to a real Provider before insert (ProviderRepository.findOneById) — missing → 404 NotFound('Provider not found'). Links FHIR Practitioner to its pre-FHIR Provider record.

items.title: BR V-PROV-002 — practitioner credentials privileged-read-only (no public/patient projection)
items.notes: FHIR Practitioner carries confidential credentials (NPI/DEA/state-license/qualification identifiers). Every read path gated admin|clinician|support|practitioner:owner — NO public/patient route. Handler returns full record verbatim (no redaction): confidentiality depends ENTIRELY on the route role gate. Patient caller → 403; unauthenticated → 401. PHI/confidentiality invariant.

items.title: BR — createPractitionerRole requires an existing Practitioner (FK guard)
items.notes: createPractitionerRole validates the supplied practitionerId resolves to a real Practitioner before insert — missing → 404 NotFound('Practitioner not found'). Links FHIR PractitionerRole to the practitioner row.

items.title: BR V-PROV-001 — provider profile is self-service (session user only)
items.notes: POST /providers creates only for the AUTHENTICATED user — person derived server-side, demographics not in body schema, so cannot create for someone else (foreign person id smuggled in body is ignored). 2nd create for same person → 409 PROVIDER_EXISTS. On success the 'provider' role is granted (addUserRole) and the current session is revoked to force re-auth. Inter-module + patient-safety/identity invariant.

items.title: BR — all provider/practitioner ops require an authenticated session (401 floor)
items.notes: Every handler enforces a session floor (UnauthorizedError → 401) in addition to the route role gate; unauthenticated request → 401 even when the target resource exists (no anonymous read of credentials).

items.title: BR V-PROV-004 — deactivate is a soft delete, records retained
items.notes: DELETE practitioner / practitioner-role are SOFT deletes — set active=false + deactivatedAt=now(), return 204; row retained for audit/legal (FHIR active flag). Non-existent id → 404 (never silent 204). Verified by repo deactivateById + handler 404 guard.

items.title: BR V-PROV-003 — practitioner / role writes are admin/credentialing role-gated
items.notes: Create/update practitioners + practitioner-roles requires admin|credentialing; deactivate requires admin. Route authMiddleware is the security boundary. Patient caller → 403 on create/update/deactivate. practitioner:owner is non-gating/aspirational (never granted; handlers do no ownership check; schema has no person link) — surfaced as latent spec drift, not exploitable today.

items.title: Appointment retention eligibility filters on scheduledAt (1yr, V-DG-003)
items.notes: V-DG-003 / DATA_GOVERNANCE §2: '1 year from date' = the appointment DATE, so the appointment target's findEligible filters on scheduledAt (not createdAt) via findArchivableAppointmentSubjects. Default seeded period = 365 days, action archive. retention-targets.ts:56-66; retention-defaults.ts:46.

items.title: Disabled policies are skipped (defensive even though findEnabled filters)
items.notes: Engine guards !policy.enabled -> outcome disabled (no target query, no audit). Defense-in-depth alongside repo.findEnabled({enabled:true}). retention-engine.ts:141-145.

items.title: Dry-run by default; live archival is env-gated opt-in
items.notes: SAFETY INVARIANT 1. Engine defaults options.dryRun ?? true; real mutation only on explicit dryRun:false. The cron gates that on RETENTION_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED==='true' (anything else -> dry-run). isRetentionEnforcementEnabled() duplicated in retention-status.ts and jobs/index.ts. retention-engine.ts:118; jobs/index.ts:13. Patient-data-loss prevention => full layers.

items.title: Every real evaluation writes a compliance audit event
items.notes: SAFETY INVARIANT 5. writeAudit appends via injected audit writer (defaults to append-only logAuditEvent) for protected_skip, dry_run, and enforced (actioned>0) outcomes. Actor = RETENTION_SYSTEM_ACTOR sentinel UUID 00000000-0000-4000-8000-0000000000d6; eventType 'compliance'; resourceType 'retention_policy'; metadata carries counts/cutoff/actions/dryRun/outcome. These are the events summarizeRetentionEnforcement reads back. retention-engine.ts:228-258.

items.title: Legally-held records always excluded (legalHoldExempt never a bypass)
items.notes: SAFETY INVARIANT 4. Each target flags candidates whose owning Person has an ACTIVE hold via personsUnderLegalHold (real dental_legal_hold store); engine filters eligible = candidates without legalHold and records legalHeldCount. The policy row's legalHoldExempt flag is NEVER consulted as a bypass. retention-engine.ts:181-184; retention-targets.ts:41,60. Cross-module + clinical => full layers.

items.title: Protected targets and 'retain' action never queried/actioned
items.notes: SAFETY INVARIANT 2. action:'retain' is refused outright (outcome protected-skip, audited retention.protected_skip) before any target lookup. A target marked protected:true (the 'audit' target) short-circuits before any read or write. Audit trail is append-only and never purged (HIPAA 7y). retention-engine.ts:147-171; retention-targets.ts:74-83.

items.title: One policy per (tenant, branch, entityType)
items.notes: uniqueIndex dental_retention_policy_tenant_entity_uq on (tenantId, branchId, entityType). branchId nullable; NULL branch = tenant-wide default, and Postgres treats NULLs as distinct so a tenant-wide row and per-branch rows coexist. retention-policy.schema.ts:61-65.

items.title: Default seeded enabled only when a real enforcement target is wired (V-RET-002)
items.notes: Enablement DERIVED from SUPPORTED_RETENTION_ENTITY_TYPES: isDefaultEnabled(entityType) -> seeded enabled only for attachment/appointment/audit-having targets; targetless types (clinical/visit/prescription) seeded enabled:false with NO_TARGET_SUFFIX note so the declared intent is visible but never routes to a silent no-target skip. Closes V-RET-002. retention-defaults.ts:54-56,84-93.

items.title: Seeding is idempotent and never clobbers operator edits
items.notes: A default is skipped if a tenant-wide (branchId NULL) row already exists for that entityType, so re-running never duplicates and never overwrites edited periods/actions. retention-defaults.ts:72-82.

items.title: 'delete' action downgraded to soft-archive (no hard-delete path)
items.notes: SAFETY INVARIANT 3. effectiveActionFor downgrades archive|delete -> archive; anonymize only when the target implements anonymize() else -> archive. The engine has no hard-delete code path; targets only ever set deletedAt. retention-engine.ts:103-109,200-207. Irreversible-data-loss prevention => full layers.

items.title: Retention status is admin-only (non-admin -> 403)
items.notes: Defense-in-depth: generated route gates roles:['admin'] via authMiddleware AND the handler re-checks user.role==='admin' (ForbiddenError) plus unauthenticated -> UnauthorizedError. Mirrors EM-DG-RBAC platform-admin (cross-tenant) governance scope used by erasure/legal-hold handlers. getRetentionStatus.ts:22-25.

items.title: Entity-type -> target resolution; no-target policies skipped
items.notes: Engine maps free-text policy.entityType to RETENTION_TARGETS (attachment, appointment, audit). An entityType with no registered target -> outcome no-target, warn-logged only, no audit noise, never actioned. SUPPORTED_RETENTION_ENTITY_TYPES derives which defaults seed enabled. retention-engine.ts:154-164; retention-targets.ts:85-92. Internal evaluation rule (no money/clinical mutation on this branch) => be-unit+fe-unit.

items.title: Delete only own review (or admin)
items.notes: deleteReview throws ForbiddenError 'You can only delete your own reviews' when !isAdmin && review.reviewer !== userId. Route-level auth also restricts to roles review:owner + admin. Ownership guard on a destructive (hard-delete) op.

items.title: One review per (context, reviewer, reviewType)
items.notes: createReview calls repo.reviewExists(context, reviewer=userId, reviewType) and throws ConflictError if a review already exists. Backed at DB by unique constraint reviews_context_reviewer_type_unique (context_id, reviewer_id, review_type). Idempotency/uniqueness guard.

items.title: Comment <=1000, reviewType <=50
items.notes: comment max 1000 chars and reviewType max 50 chars enforced at validator (z.string().max(...)) AND at DB via check constraints reviews_comment_check / reviews_review_type_check. context + reviewedEntity must be valid UUIDs.

items.title: NPS score must be 0-10
items.notes: npsScore validated at zValidator (z.number().int().gte(0).lte(10)) AND enforced at DB via check constraint reviews_nps_score_check (npsScore >= 0 AND <= 10). Out-of-range rejected before handler runs.

items.title: Get-review access: reviewer | reviewedEntity | admin
items.notes: getReview throws ForbiddenError unless isAdmin OR repo.canUserAccessReview(review, userId) (reviewer === userId OR reviewedEntity === userId). Authorization guard for a single review.

items.title: List-review scoping for non-admins
items.notes: listReviews: non-admin requesting reviewer filter !== self → 403; reviewedEntity filter !== self → 403; with no reviewer/reviewedEntity filter, defaults filters.reviewer = self (cannot enumerate others' reviews). Admins bypass scoping. Tenancy/authz guard.

items.title: Cannot review yourself
items.notes: createReview throws ValidationError 'Cannot review yourself' when body.reviewedEntity === session.user.id. Prevents a reviewer from being the reviewed entity.

items.title: Surface double-booking warning on create
items.notes: V-SCH-005 / AC-SCH-001: when create response includes warnings:['DOUBLE_BOOKING'] the booking still succeeds but the modal stays open with an amber soft-warning and a Done button instead of auto-closing. extractDoubleBookingWarning parses the marker.

items.title: Working hours validation (start before end per open day)
items.notes: validateWorkingHours (working-hours.logic.ts validateWorkingHoursLogic) returns per-day errors (e.g. start must be before end on open days); UI re-labels error keys to day labels (Monday, ...). Blocks save.

items.title: Working hours validation (start before end per open day)
items.notes: validateWorkingHours (working-hours.logic.ts validateWorkingHoursLogic) returns per-day errors (e.g. start must be before end on open days); UI re-labels error keys to day labels (Monday, ...). Blocks save.

items.title: Owner cannot be deactivated / non-owner cannot deactivate
items.notes: canDeactivate: false unless current user is dentist_owner AND target is not dentist_owner. Owner row shows '(Owner -- cannot deactivate)' caption and no Deactivate control; backend enforces owner-only + last-owner protection server-side.

items.title: Owner cannot be deactivated / non-owner cannot deactivate

items.notes: Route guard requireRole('staff') + StaffPage renders StaffAccessDenied for any role !== dentist_owner. Pure FE RBAC gate (rbac.canAccess staff:true only for owner); backend createMember/deactivate/update are owner-gated server-side (403 non-owner).

items.title: File status FSM (uploading → processing → available / failed)
items.notes: file_status enum: uploading|processing|available|failed (file.schema.ts). complete is only valid from 'uploading' (else ValidationError '<status> status, cannot complete upload'); verification miss / abort → 'failed'; only 'available' files yield download URLs. Download (strict) rejects non-available with FILE_NOT_AVAILABLE.

items.title: HIPAA audit logging on file access/list/delete
items.notes: getFile, listFiles and deleteFile emit audit events via the injected audit service (category 'hipaa', eventType data-access/data-modification, captures ip/user-agent/filename/size/mime). Audit-write failure is caught and logged — never blocks the primary operation.

items.title: Active-multipart-upload guard (multipartUploadId required)
items.notes: part-url/complete/abort all require file.multipartUploadId to be set (else ValidationError 'No active multipart upload for this file'). The S3 uploadId persisted at initiate is the gate; cleared on complete (success) and abort. complete additionally requires ≥1 part.

items.title: Multipart owner-check on part-url/complete/abort
items.notes: P1 security fix: a non-owner user is rejected (ForbiddenError) from generating part URLs, completing, or aborting another user's in-progress multipart upload. Covered by multipartOwnerCheck.test.ts.

items.title: Access guard: owner-or-admin on read/download/delete
items.notes: Handler-level authz: file.owner === user.id OR userHasRole(admin); otherwise ForbiddenError 'You can only access/download/delete your own files'. listFiles enforces the same by force-setting filter owner=user.id for non-admins. Defense-in-depth on top of route-level authMiddleware roles (user:owner / admin).

items.title: Per-MIME upload size ceiling (image 100MB / DICOM cap, hard cap clamp)
items.notes: P2-7: maxUploadSizeForMime — images stay at config.maxFileSizeBytes (default 100MB); application/dicom (CBCT) gets the higher dicomMaxFileSizeBytes, both clamped to the absolute hard cap. size > ceiling → ValidationError 'File size exceeds maximum limit of <ceiling>'. Enforced in BOTH uploadFile and initiateMultipartUpload; falls back to 100MB when config absent. core/storage.ts maxUploadSizeForMime/formatByteCeiling. Relates to imaging BR 'max 100MB delegated to multipart layer'.

items.title: Upload-init DB row rollback on storage failure
items.notes: Both uploadFile and initiateMultipartUpload create the stored_file row BEFORE calling the storage provider; if the provider (presign / initiate / persist uploadId) throws, the just-created row is deleted (deleteOneById) so no orphaned 'uploading' rows are left. Cleanup failure is logged, original error re-thrown.

userEntryPoint.control: New Visit card (no branchId/memberId in org context store)

items.title: Workspace route tree requires an active, unlocked, un-expired PIN session
items.notes: _workspace.tsx beforeLoad: requireAuth + pinSession.getSession()/isExpired()/isLocked(); on miss redirect to /auth/pin-select. Then loadOrgContext() seeds branch/member/role. Covered by _workspace.pin-guard.test.ts. Gates every workspace use-case below. No write.

items.title: Completed/locked visits render the workspace read-only
items.notes: isReadOnly = status completed|locked → ToothSlideout readOnly, ApplyTemplateButton hidden, PaymentSummaryBar read-only, Share-PMD shown. Prevents post-completion edits to a finalized clinical record. FE gate (backend is the hard guarantee).

items.title: Completed/locked visits render the workspace read-only
items.notes: isReadOnly = status completed|locked → ToothSlideout readOnly, ApplyTemplateButton hidden, PaymentSummaryBar read-only, Share-PMD shown. Prevents post-completion edits to a finalized clinical record. FE gate (backend is the hard guarantee).

items.title: Top-bar write affordances hidden by role (Rx/Consent/Plan/Lab/PMD)
items.notes: E2/FIX-008: canPrescribe/canCaptureConsent/canAddTreatment gate which write-launcher icons render. Notes + Attachments deliberately NOT role-hidden (view/supervised-draft entry points). Backend 403 is the hard guarantee. FE affordance honesty rule.

items.title: Top-bar write affordances hidden by role (Rx/Consent/Plan/Lab/PMD)
items.notes: E2/FIX-008: canPrescribe/canCaptureConsent/canAddTreatment gate which write-launcher icons render. Notes + Attachments deliberately NOT role-hidden (view/supervised-draft entry points). Backend 403 is the hard guarantee. FE affordance honesty rule.

items.title: End video call (admin only)
items.notes: endVideoCall: admin force-ends the active call. Computes durationMinutes (differenceInMinutes startedAt->now), sets status 'ended' + endedAt + durationMinutes, clears room.activeVideoCallMessage, notifies in-call participants (comms.video-call-ended), appends system message and updates room lastMessageAt. Guards: room exists (404), caller is participant (403), caller is room admin (403 'Only room admin can end video calls'), active call exists (404). Returns 200 VideoCallEndResponse (callDuration, systemMessage).

items.title: Video-call lifecycle fans out notifs to participants
items.notes: Cross-module flow: comms calls the notifs facade (ctx.get('notifs').createNotification) for in-app notifications — types comms.video-call-started (start, to other participants), comms.video-call-joined (join, to active participants), comms.video-call-left (leave, to remaining), comms.video-call-ended (end, to in-call participants). relatedEntityType 'chat-room', consentValidated true (room participation implies consent). Delivery is best-effort: per-recipient failures are caught + logged and never abort the call action.

items.title: Join active video call
items.notes: joinVideoCall: adds caller as a participant to the active call, transitions call status 'starting'->'active' (generating WebRTC roomUrl + token on first join), fans out comms.video-call-joined notifs to other active participants, and appends a 'joined' system message. Guards: displayName required (ValidationError), room exists (404), caller is participant (403), active call exists (404), not already joined-and-not-left (409 ConflictError). audioEnabled/videoEnabled default true. Returns 200 VideoCallJoinResponse (roomUrl/token/callStatus/participants). roomUrl is ws/wss signaling endpoint; token sentinel USE_SESSION_TOKEN.

items.title: Leave video call (auto-end on empty)
items.notes: leaveVideoCall: marks caller's participant leftAt, notifies remaining participants (comms.video-call-left), appends 'left' system message. AUTO-END: if no active participants remain, sets call status 'ended' + endedAt, clears room.activeVideoCallMessage, appends 'Video call ended (no participants remaining)' system message. Guards: room exists (404), caller is participant (403), active call exists (404), caller is in call (404), caller has not already left (404). Returns 200 LeaveVideoCallResponse (callStillActive, remainingParticipants).

items.title: Invoice domain events (InvoiceCreated / InvoicePaid)
items.notes: domain-events.ts: DE-020 InvoiceCreated emitted post-create (best-effort, non-blocking, pg-boss queue dental.billing.domain-events); DE-021 InvoicePaid satisfied by audit-log row (ADR-006: no event bus) on fully-paid transition. Tests: dental-billing-events.test.ts.

items.title: Coverage authorization (LOA) lifecycle backing claims (inter-module)
items.notes: dental_coverage_authorization (LOA) is owned by dental-patient (createCoverageAuthorization/updateCoverageAuthorizationStatus/listCoverageAuthorizations) but is the dependency that drives billing claim coverage (BR-056 validity gate, BR-057 coverage cap). Billing reads it via insurance-billing.facade getCoverageAuthorizationForBilling. Routes co-registered with the revenue-cycle smoke test.

items.title: Subject/tenancy + legal-hold resolved through cross-module facades
items.notes: requestErasure resolves the subject via getErasureSubjectPatient (patient-erasure.facade) and branch→org via getBranchOrganizationId (org-erasure.facade) to own tenancy; approveErasure consults isPersonUnderLegalHold (legal-hold.facade) under the shared ns 1003 lock to enforce the hold-blocks-erasure invariant. Cross-module read flows that gate the erasure decision.

items.title: Link image to treatment plan / ortho case / report
items.notes: G5b loose-coupled link: targetId references another module's row by UUID with NO DB FK. linkType in {treatment_plan, ortho_case, report}. Idempotent: linking same (image,type,target) returns existing link. targetId UUID-validated.

items.title: Imaging tier resolution via dental-org facade (getImagingTierForBranch)
items.notes: All tier gates call org-imaging.facade (getImagingTierForBranch / getOrgDataForBranch). createCephReport also reads branchName for the snapshot. Module-boundary-compliant facade call (no direct cross-module repo import).

items.title: WF-099: imaging → storage (S3/MinIO) for radiograph objects
items.notes: Presigned PUT / S3 multipart (initiate + per-part URLs) on upload; presigned GET download URLs for viewing/CBCT handoff. Loose sync coupling keyed on studyId/fileId.

items.title: WF-091: imaging context from visit (UUID loose coupling)
items.notes: Study/finding carry visitId (UUID-only, no DB FK) propagated to findings from study.visitId. Loose coupling.

items.title: Link treatment item to appointment
items.notes: P1-21: loose-ref link (no hard FK). appointmentExistsForPatient cross-check (ValidationError if not for patient — appointment-link.facade, cross-module). assertPatientBranchAccess; archived blocked; treatment 404 if missing.

items.title: Inter-module: treatment plan → scheduling link
items.notes: appointment-link.facade verifies the appointment exists for the patient (loose UUID ref, no hard FK) so the plan can flow to the calendar (proposed→scheduled→done).

items.title: Inter-module: visit resolution + lock state via dental-visit facade
items.notes: getVisitOrThrow (visit.service) and getVisitForPerio (visit-perio.facade) resolve the parent visit and its status — driving branch/patient binding, immutability gates, and the lock cascade. Cross-module dependency on dental-visit lifecycle.

items.title: Auto-generate PMD on visit completion (failure-isolated trigger)

userEntryPoint.control: Top bar Complete visit icon → PreCompletionChecklist Confirm

items.notes: Drives draft->active->completed->locked. Locked block; transition-map validation; completed chiefComplaint edit block; active re-check ACTIVE_VISIT_EXISTS; completion guards (open treatments, signed consent, notes); per-transition audit (checked_in/complete fail-closed/locked); auto-PMD on completion (failure-isolated dynamic import); optional auto-discard behind flag. Each terminal write runs withTenantTx.

items.title: Portal reads invoices/balance via dental-billing facade (boundary lint)
items.notes: Cross-module flow: listMyInvoices and getMyBalance call getInvoicesByPatientId (dental-billing/repos/billing-dental-patient.facade.ts) — the portal consumes only this facade, never the dental-billing schema directly (Phase 10 boundary lint). Facade scopes by eq(patientId), newest-first; portal applies voided/uncollectible filtering and money roll-up on top. Money + inter-module → full layer set.

items.title: Appointment check-in → visit creation (WF-007)

userEntryPoint.control: Check In hover button on appointment card (aria-label Check in <patient>)

items.notes: checkInAppointment. Atomically (withTenantTx([branchId])): set status=checked_in + create draft DentalVisit (createVisit) + link visitId back to appointment, returns {appointment, visitId}. DE-001 VisitCheckedIn marker is OWNED by dental-visit (createVisit writes the audit row); no event bus per ADR-006. PP-3: auto-enqueue patient on queue board ('waiting') after commit, best-effort. Facade: findInProgressVisitByPatient/createVisit (dental-visit).

items.title: Domain events: AppointmentBooked (DE-010) / AppointmentCancelled (DE-011)
items.notes: emitAppointmentBooked / emitAppointmentCancelled enqueue to the shared pg-boss queue 'dental.scheduling.domain-events' (best-effort, never throws → no rollback of the booking/cancel). Payload = {event, appointmentId, patientId, branchId}. Consumed asynchronously by any registered consumer (ADR-006: no synchronous event bus).

items.title: Inter-module: dental-clinical consent/attachment facade gates visit lifecycle
items.notes: hasSignedConsentForVisit + countAttachmentsForVisit (clinical-visit.facade) feed completion gating (consent required), perform gating (consent required), and discard safety (refuse if signed consent / attachments exist).

items.title: Inter-module: dental-org facade for branch fee overrides, procedure catalog, membership, and audit tenant resolution
items.notes: org-billing.facade provides getBranchFeeOverrides + getActiveProcedureCode (fee defaulting), getActiveMembershipId (notes authorMemberId), getBranchOrgId (resolve organizationId tenant for every dental_audit_log row); assertOrgLive blocks PHI on provisional clinics.

items.title: Inter-module: provider/patient/person resolution via facades
items.notes: EMR never reaches into patient/provider/person schemas directly (MODULE_BOUNDARIES EX-005/EX-006). Uses provider-emr.facade (getProviderForEMR, getProviderByPersonIdForEMR, getProviderWithPersonForEMR) and patient-emr.facade (getPatientForEMR, getPatientByPersonIdForEMR, getPatientWithPersonForEMR, listPatientsForEMR, listPatientsWithPersonForEMR) for existence/ownership resolution + expand composition.

items.title: Route a clicked push to its in-app deep link

userEntryPoint.control: OS/browser push notification click (OneSignal click listener)

items.notes: makeNotificationClickHandler reads push additionalData {type,relatedEntity,notificationId} and navigates: appointment/booking->/calendar, recall->/patients, invoice/payment/billing->/billing, else->/dashboard (never a dead tap). Single module-guarded OneSignal click listener for the page lifetime. Crosses notifications -> scheduling/billing/patients modules. No app backend endpoint (payload comes from the push).

items.title: Open patient clinical workspace (card click)
items.notes: Navigation only (navigate to /$patientId — the _workspace route). No direct endpoint; the destination loads the clinical workspace (separate feature). Cross-module entry point from the patient list into clinical.

items.title: Inter-module: findBillingParty — invoice merchant/customer existence validation
items.notes: person-billing.facade.findBillingParty: billing handlers (create/update/finalize/void/delete/pay/capture/refund/markUncollectible invoice, create/onboard merchant) resolve the merchant/customer Person before mutating money/invoices. Money + cross-module → full layer set.

items.title: Inter-module: ensurePersonForUser / findPersonById — patient & provider provisioning bridge
items.notes: person-provisioning.facade: find-or-create the backing Person before a patient/provider row is created. ensurePersonForUser forces person.id = user.id (security), seeds firstName from user.name || 'Anonymous' when no input, and updates PII if input supplied on an existing person. Consumed by patient/createPatient.ts and provider/createProvider.ts.

items.title: Default policies seeded during org provisioning (cross-module)
items.notes: dental-org createOnboarding.ts:205 and DentalOrganizationManagement_create.ts:64 call seedDefaultRetentionPolicies(db, orgId, {createdBy:user.id}) best-effort during provisioning so a new org starts with the conservative-default registry. Cross-module org-lifecycle flow => full layers (verified via org onboarding endpoints, not a retention route).

items.title: Erasure: confirm genuine object-store keys before S3 delete (facade)
items.notes: filterExistingStoredFileIds(db, candidateIds) → subset that are real stored_file rows. Non-UUID candidates dropped up front (column is uuid; avoids Postgres cast error). Called from dental-clinical/attachment-erasure.facade.ts to confirm which dental_attachment.filePath values are genuine object-store keys before issuing an S3 delete — so a legacy free-form path (e.g. /uploads/x.jpg) is never handed to the storage client as a silently-succeeding bogus delete.

items.title: Get invoice by id
items.notes: getInvoice.ts. Returns invoice with line items. Read-only → be-unit. WF-051 [INFERRED] conceptual (dental-billing invoice detail read).

items.title: Get merchant account (supports 'me')
items.notes: getMerchantAccount.ts. Resolves 'me' to current user's account; owner-only read (person !== user.id → 403). Skips authz for internal expand requests (already authorized at parent). Read-only → be-unit.

items.title: List invoices (filter + pagination)
items.notes: listInvoices.ts. Filters customer/merchant/status/context; parsePagination default limit 25 max 100. Read-only → be-unit.

items.title: Get a single booking
items.notes: Read by id; 404 if missing. Authorization: requester must be the client OR host of the booking (checkBookingOwnership) else 403. Supports expand of host/client/slot via middleware.

items.title: Get booking event (public, supports 'me')
items.notes: Public discovery read by id; 404 if missing. Special 'me' id resolves to the current user's first owned event (requires auth -> 401 if anonymous, 404 if no events). No ownership gate on a concrete id (events are publicly discoverable).

items.title: Get schedule exception
items.notes: Read by id; 404 if missing. Owner-only (exception.owner === user.id else 403).

items.title: Get a single time slot (public)
items.notes: Public endpoint, no auth. Read by id; 404 if missing. Supports expand via middleware.

items.title: List bookings (own as client or host)
items.notes: Ownership-scoped list. Explicit host/client filter must equal user.id else 403. With no host/client filter, results limited to bookings where user is client OR host (clientOrHost). Supports status filter, startDate/endDate range, pagination (default 25, max 100), ordered by scheduledAt desc.

items.title: List slots for an event (availability)
items.notes: Optional auth. 404 if event missing. Defaults: time window now -> +7 days, status='available'. Returns the slots that clients can book.

items.title: List booking events (discovery / search)
items.notes: Public discovery list, defaults to status='active'. Supports context, effectiveDate, full-text q (title+description+keywords), and tags filtering with dual semantics: CSV ?tags=a,b => OR; repeated ?tags=a&tags=b => AND. Paginated.

items.title: List schedule exceptions for an event
items.notes: 404 if event missing. Authorization via checkBookingEventOwnership (admin/support bypass; otherwise must own event). Filters: dateStart/dateEnd range, recurring (string->bool), reason. Always scoped to event.owner. Paginated, ordered by startDatetime asc.

items.title: Staff reclaims device — back to patient workspace
items.notes: Staff header navigate({to:'/$patientId'}). Pure client navigation, no endpoint → fe-unit only.

items.title: Clear the signature canvas before signing
items.notes: clear() wipes the canvas and resets hasStroke→false, re-disabling Accept & Sign. Pure FE interaction, no endpoint → fe-unit only.

items.title: Get chat messages (paginated)
items.notes: getChatMessages: read messages for a room with messageType filter (text|system|video_call) and pagination (default pageSize 50). Verifies room exists (404) and caller is a participant (403) before returning. Read-only.

items.title: Get chat room
items.notes: getChatRoom: read-only fetch of a single room. 404 NotFoundError if missing; 403 ForbiddenError if caller is not a participant. Read-only access restricted to participants.

items.title: Get WebRTC ICE/TURN/STUN servers
items.notes: getIceServers: returns config.webrtc.iceServers for WebRTC peer connection setup. Static config read; no DB, no per-room authorization (BaseContext, not participant-scoped).

items.title: List user's chat rooms
items.notes: listChatRooms: returns only rooms where caller's Person ID is a participant (findUserChatRooms via withParticipant filter). Supports status/context/hasActiveCall/withParticipant filters and page/pageSize/offset/limit pagination (default pageSize 50). withParticipant intersects caller's rooms with the named participant; sorted by lastMessageAt desc.

items.title: Read-only HMO coverage estimate (no persistence)
items.notes: estimateClaimCoverage. Pure engine split covered vs patient portion; no persistence. Cash request (no profile) → fully patient-pay. P1-4 cross-tenant guard: caller-supplied patientId/insuranceProfileId resolved + assertPatientBranchAccess BEFORE any coverage read (facade runs on RLS-bypassing conn). Tests: estimateClaimCoverage.cross-tenant.test.ts, utils/coverage-estimate.test.ts.

items.title: AR aging buckets per patient + summary
items.notes: getArAging (P2-14). Buckets non-voided invoice balances current/30/60/90+ by due date (fallback issue/created) vs asOf, per patient + practice summary. EM-BIL-002 scoping. computePatientAging/computeAgingSummary (utils/aging). Tests: dental-billing.ar-aging-statements.test.ts.

items.title: Get insurance claim with lines
items.notes: getInsuranceClaim. Cross-tenant isolation: a non-member of the claim's branch sees 404 (branch-access denial converted to NotFound). Read-only.

items.title: AR KPI dashboard (DSO, collection rate, write-offs)
items.notes: getCollectionsKpis (Phase 3.1). DSO, collection rate, write-off total, outstanding AR + aging breakdown from non-voided invoices. EM-BIL-002 scoping. computeBillingKpis (utils/kpis). Tests: dental-billing.kpis.test.ts, utils/kpis.test.ts.

items.title: Collections summary by time window
items.notes: getCollectionsSummary (FR4.5). Totals per window (today/month/year or from/to). EM-BIL-002 branch scoping. Read-only.

items.title: Collections worklist (overdue accounts, most-aged first)
items.notes: getCollectionsWorklist (Phase 2.4/BR-051). One row per patient with overdue balance: totalOverdueCents, openInvoiceCount, oldestDaysOverdue, hasActivePlan (patients.hasActivePaymentPlan), last-contact enrichment from dental_collection_note. Sorted most-aged first. EM-BIL-002 scoping. Tests: dental-billing.collections-worklist.test.ts.

items.title: Get invoice with line items + payments
items.notes: getDentalInvoice. Branch-access authz (404 on miss). Resolves patientName (patient facade) + visitDate (dental-visit facade). Read-only.

items.title: Get per-patient outstanding balance
items.notes: getPatientBalance (FR4.4). Sums non-voided invoice balances + overdue + active-plan count. Branch authz via patient preferredBranch; reads scoped to caller active branches in withTenantTx (never sums foreign branches). Tests: dental-billing.patient-balance-coherence.test.ts.

items.title: Get patient credit ledger + available balance
items.notes: getPatientCredits (Phase 4.1). Ledger rows + available balance (SUM of signed rows). Credits are a patient-global wallet (across branches); branch authz via patient preferredBranch gates access. assertPatientBranchAccess.

items.title: AR-by-payer aging worklist
items.notes: getPayerArAging (P1-26). Outstanding claims aged by submission date current/31-60/61-90/90+ grouped by payer. EM-BIL-002 scoping. Insurer names via getInsurerNamesForBilling (dental-patient). computePayerAging (utils/aging).

items.title: Get payment plan with installments
items.notes: getDentalPaymentPlan. Branch authz via invoice; plan-not-found → 404. Read-only.

items.title: Get BIR payment receipt (Official Receipt)

userEntryPoint.control: 'Receipt' button on a payment row -> PaymentReceipt overlay (PrintableDocument)

items.notes: getDentalPaymentReceipt (FR4.6). Returns BIR OR fields from branch settings (getBranchBirInfo): registeredName/businessStyle/TIN/address, OR number (=receiptNumber), VAT breakdown (from BR-054 invoice tax), correct non-VAT/VAT statement. Works for voided payments (EC5). Tests: dental-billing.receipt-bir.test.ts.

items.title: Insurance claim worklist (filtered)
items.notes: listInsuranceClaims (P1-26). Filter by branch/status/insuranceProfileId(payer)/patient. EM-BIL-002 scoping (omitted branchId → caller active branches). Read in withTenantTx.
